title: Observability: structured logging and request correlation
description:
Add structured HTTP/service logging and request correlation across the backend. This issue is about making request flows and background event handling diagnosable, not about redefining `/health` semantics.

## Scope
- [ ] Standardize structured request/response logging for the gateway and backend services.
- [ ] Propagate a correlation/request ID across gateway, service, and background message-handling boundaries where possible.
- [ ] Ensure HTTP logs consistently include route, method, status, latency, and relevant entity/user identifiers when safe.
- [ ] Ensure NATS/background-handler logs include subject, consumer/subscriber identity, message/correlation IDs, retry outcome, and failure reason.
- [ ] Document local and CI log expectations so failing tests and demos are easier to diagnose.

## Non-Goals
- [ ] `/health` payload truthfulness belongs to winzy.ai-2m3.
- [ ] Larger event-trace propagation changes should be tracked separately if they become necessary.

## Notes
- Good logging should make security bugs, integration failures, and flaky tests faster to understand.
- Keep personally sensitive data out of logs.
acceptance_criteria:
Gateway and backend services emit structured HTTP and background-handler logs with usable request/correlation context.
Main request and event-processing paths can be followed across gateway, service, and consumer boundaries during debugging and failing tests.
The issue stays narrowly focused on observability/correlation rather than absorbing unrelated health-check or user-copy concerns.
close_reason: Structured logging and correlation ID propagation added across all services

title: Fix CI: override vulnerable transitive Microsoft.OpenApi 2.0.0 (NU1903)
description: GHSA-v5pm-xwqc-g5wc published for Microsoft.OpenApi <=2.7.4; NuGet audit treats it as error (NU1903), breaking restore in ci-backend and ci-e2e on main. AspNetCore.OpenApi 10.x pins minimum 2.0.0, so added direct PackageReference Microsoft.OpenApi 2.7.5 in all six service csprojs.
close_reason: Direct Microsoft.OpenApi 2.7.5 reference added to all six services; restore/build/format clean locally.

title: Service health wiring is inconsistent: auth requires INatsConnection that is never registered
description: auth-service adds AddNatsHealthCheck() but no service registers AddNatsMessaging(), so /health will attempt to construct NatsHealthCheck without an INatsConnection. At the same time, the other services still expose bare health checks without DB/NATS probes, so the gateway's aggregate health remains misleading. Fix service startup wiring and align all services with the documented health contract.
acceptance_criteria: Backend services either register NATS messaging before NATS health probes or intentionally omit those probes where NATS is not in scope, and /health wiring is consistent with the documented contract.\nTests cover service startup, per-service health responses, and at least one aggregated-health path with realistic dependency wiring.\nLogs make missing INatsConnection registration or broken dependency probes obvious instead of failing mysteriously.
close_reason: done

title: Backend rewrite: single Go service replaces .NET microservices
description:
Rewrite the backend as ONE Go service, replacing 7 .NET microservices + NATS JetStream + 6 Postgres databases. Decided 2026-07-09: the app is a solo MVP (owner + a few friends); the distributed architecture was a school requirement (M321 Verteilte Systeme) and taxes every feature change.

WHY (for future selves): every cross-cutting feature currently touches 3+ services, contracts, and NATS events; local dev needs a 14-container compose stack; CI/pre-push cycles are minutes long; Railway runs 13 services + 7 volumes for ~5 users. The rewrite optimizes for the actual goal: fast feature churn by one person with AI coding agents. Go was chosen over TypeScript (owner's call, 2026-07-09) for the single-static-binary deploy, agent-friendly simplicity, and because the owner wants to learn it. ALL features are kept ("The feature set is good and I am still testing").

TARGET ARCHITECTURE
- One Go module in backend/: stdlib net/http (Go 1.22+ routing), pgx v5 (+sqlc where it helps), ONE Postgres database, slog. No framework.
- Internal module boundaries mirror the old services (auth, habits, social, challenges, notifications, activity) as Go packages; the old /internal/* HTTP endpoints are NOT ported - they become direct function calls.
- NATS is replaced by an in-process event hook registry (defined in winzy.ai-rdc7.2): modules register handlers for events like userDeleted, habitCompleted, visibilityChanged. What JetStream durability bought us (guaranteed cascade processing) is now bought by running cascades in the same transaction or process.
- The Go binary also serves the Expo web bundle same-origin (static + SPA fallback), replacing the gateway pattern. JWT validation + rate limiting + CORS move from the YARP gateway into middleware.
- Same public endpoint list as today (see ground-truth comment): /auth/register|login|refresh, /habits/public/**, /social/witness/{token}, /notifications/vapid-public-key. Everything else requires a valid JWT.
- Compatibility contract with the untouched frontend: response shapes per the C# contracts, error bodies 409 {"error": "..."} and 422 {"errors": {field: [msgs]}}, auth response {accessToken, refreshToken, user} + httpOnly refresh cookie. The frontend never decodes the JWT, so JWT internals are free.
- Railway shrinks to 1 app + 1 Postgres. The existing api-gateway Railway service gets REPURPOSED to run the Go image so the public URL (api-gateway-production-9faf.up.railway.app) never changes.

GROUND TRUTH: two exploration reports (2026-07-09) are attached as comments on this epic - a full backend inventory (endpoints, data models, NATS events, flame algorithm, auth mechanics, config) and a frontend integration inventory (API client behavior, auth on client, push flow, E2E harness, web serving). Child beads cite them; when in doubt, the C# source is the final spec.

NOTABLE SCOPE FACTS discovered during planning:
- Challenge templates are frontend-only presets - no backend artifact exists, nothing to port.
- Honest Minimums + Flame Promises live in habit-service; Witness Links live in social-service.
- Activity-feed display names: the 3-path enrichment + backfill job collapses to a read-time SQL join in a single DB.
- Refresh tokens will NOT be migrated (users re-login once; decision in winzy.ai-rdc7.9). Password hashes and VAPID keys migrate verbatim so logins and push subscriptions survive.

STRUCTURE: children 1-12; 3 and 8 are split into sub-beads. Critical path: 1 -> 2 -> 3.x -> (4,5,6,7 parallel-capable) -> 8.x -> 9 -> 10 -> 11, with 12 (parity) feeding 10. Behavioral parity is proven by winzy.ai-rdc7.12 before cutover; the old stack stays live and untouched until then.

EXECUTION PROTOCOL (all child beads follow this)
1. The PM session (Fable) elaborates the bead just-in-time: adds/refines a detailed EXECUTION STEPS list when dispatching, informed by what has already landed.
2. The PM launches ONE worker agent per bead (Sonnet 5 default; Opus for the hardest beads, e.g. the flame engine or data migration). Parallel workers only for genuinely independent beads, each in an isolated worktree.
3. The worker implements exactly what the bead says, follows its EXECUTION STEPS in order, runs the quality gates, self-checks every acceptance criterion, then STOPS and reports: files changed, decisions made, full test/gate output, deviations, open questions. Workers NEVER close beads, NEVER commit, NEVER push, and do not run br write commands.
4. The PM reads the bead first, reviews the diff against its acceptance criteria, sends fixes back to the same worker, and commits with the bead ID once satisfied.
5. ONLY the PM closes beads. A bead is done when the PM has verified it, not when the worker says so.
6. Pushes are batched per the git workflow.

Supersedes epic winzy.ai-70vj and bug winzy.ai-xtz1 (closed with reasons).
close_reason: Epic complete: single Go service + one Postgres fully replaced the .NET microservices — ported, parity-proven, data migrated, cut over on Railway (2026-07-13), old stack torn down (Railway + repo). Prod live and healthy.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
GROUND TRUTH 1/2 - BACKEND INVENTORY (deep-explore, 2026-07-09). The C# source remains the final spec; this is the map.

== GLOBAL CONVENTIONS ==
Every entity: Id uuid PK (gen_random_uuid), CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamptz. Postgres snake_case, one DB per service (auth_db..activity_db), migrations auto-applied on startup. Health: every service GET /health = DB + NATS check. Services trust X-User-Id header set by gateway (400 if missing).

== ENDPOINTS ==
GATEWAY (YARP, 5000 internal / 5050 external): strips client X-User-Id, sets it from JWT sub; hard-403s /internal/ and /habits/user/ paths. Rate limits: standard 300/min/IP, auth 10/min/IP. PUBLIC routes (only these skip JWT): POST /auth/register|login|refresh; GET /habits/public/**; GET /social/witness/{token}; GET /notifications/vapid-public-key.
AUTH: POST /auth/register|login|refresh (pub), /auth/logout; GET+PUT /auth/profile; PUT /auth/password (revokes all refresh tokens); DELETE /auth/account (publishes user.deleted FIRST, then deletes); GET /auth/export (1/60s/user, fans out to 5 services' /internal/export); GET /auth/users/search?q= (min 2 chars, max 20 results). Internal: resolve/{username}->userId; POST profiles {userIds[]} cap 100; export/{userId}.
HABITS: POST/GET /habits; GET/PUT/DELETE /habits/{id} (DELETE = soft archive: sets ArchivedAt, cancels active promise, publishes habit.archived); POST /habits/{id}/complete; DELETE+PUT /habits/{id}/completions/{date}; GET /habits/{id}/stats (requires X-Timezone); GET /habits/completions?date=; promise CRUD POST/GET/DELETE/PATCH /habits/{habitId}/promise + /promise/visibility. Public: GET /habits/public/{username} and /habits/public/{username}/flame.svg. Internal: export, /habits/user/{userId}, /habits/internal/{habitId}/consistency?from&to&tz.
SOCIAL: POST /social/friends/request; PUT /social/friends/request/{id}/accept (creates reverse row) | /decline; DELETE /social/friends/{friendId} (deletes both directions, publishes friend.removed); GET /social/friends?page&pageSize (enriched with profiles + flame); GET /social/friends/requests + /requests/count; GET /social/friends/{friendId}/profile; PUT /social/visibility/{habitId} (validates ownership via habit svc, publishes visibility.changed); GET /social/visibility (batch); GET+PUT /social/preferences; witness-links CRUD + POST .../{id}/rotate. Public: GET /social/witness/{token}. Internal: visible-habits/{userId}?viewer=, friends/{u1}/{u2}, friends/{userId}->friendIds[], export.
CHALLENGES: POST /challenges (validates friendship via social); GET /challenges?page&pageSize&status&since (derived-status filter); GET /challenges/{id}; PUT /challenges/{id}/claim (Completed->Claimed); DELETE /challenges/{id} (creator only). Internal: export.
NOTIFICATIONS: GET /notifications?page&pageSize; PUT /notifications/{id}/read, /read-all; GET /notifications/unread-count; PUT /notifications/settings; POST+DELETE /notifications/devices. Public: GET /notifications/vapid-public-key. Internal: export.
ACTIVITY: GET /activity/feed?cursor&limit (cursor = CreatedAt ISO; friends+self, visibility-filtered). Internal: export.

== DATA MODEL ==
auth_db: User(Email unique lowercase, Username unique lowercase, PasswordHash, DisplayName?, AvatarUrl?, LastLoginAt?); RefreshToken(UserId FK cascade, Token unique 512, ExpiresAt, RevokedAt?).
habit_db: Habit(UserId idx, Name, Icon?, Color?, Frequency Daily/Weekly/Custom as string, CustomDays? jsonb List<DayOfWeek>, MinimumDescription?, ArchivedAt?); Completion(HabitId+LocalDate UNIQUE composite, UserId idx, CompletedAt utc, LocalDate date, CompletionKind None/Full/Minimum string default Full, Note?); Promise(UserId, HabitId FK cascade, TargetConsistency double, EndDate date, PrivateNote?, Status Active/Kept/EndedBelow/Cancelled string, IsPublicOnFlame bool default false, ResolvedAt?; UNIQUE filtered (UserId,HabitId) WHERE status='Active').
social_db: Friendship(UserId, FriendId, Status Pending/Accepted; unique(UserId,FriendId); bidirectional = 2 rows); VisibilitySetting(UserId, HabitId, Visibility Private/Friends/Public; unique(UserId,HabitId)); SocialPreference(UserId unique, DefaultHabitVisibility default Private); WitnessLink(OwnerId, Token unique base64url 32 bytes, Label?, RevokedAt? null=active); WitnessLinkHabit(composite PK WitnessLinkId FK cascade + HabitId).
challenge_db: Challenge(HabitId, CreatorId, RecipientId, MilestoneType ConsistencyTarget/DaysInPeriod/TotalCompletions/CustomDateRange/ImprovementMilestone string, TargetValue double, PeriodDays int, RewardDescription, Status Active/Completed/Claimed/Cancelled/Expired string, CurrentProgress double 0-1, EndsAt, CompletedAt?, ClaimedAt?, CompletionCount int, ProcessedCompletionDates? JSON HashSet<DateOnly>, CustomStartDate?, CustomEndDate?, BaselineConsistency? double; UNIQUE filtered (CreatorId,RecipientId,HabitId) WHERE status='Active').
notification_db: Notification(UserId idx, Type string, Data jsonb, ReadAt?, IdempotencyKey? unique WHERE NOT NULL, PushDelivered bool); NotificationSettings(UserId unique, HabitReminders/FriendActivity/ChallengeUpdates bool default true); DeviceToken(UserId idx, Platform web_push/expo_push, Token text = full web-push subscription JSON, DeviceId?; unique(UserId,DeviceId) WHERE NOT NULL).
activity_db: FeedEntry(ActorId idx, ActorUsername?, ActorDisplayName?, EventType string, Data jsonb, IdempotencyKey? unique WHERE NOT NULL, DeletedAt? soft-delete with global filter).

== NATS EVENTS (become in-process hooks) ==
Streams USERS/HABITS/FRIENDS/CHALLENGES/VISIBILITY over subjects user.>, habit.>, friend.>, challenge.>, visibility.> (no prefix). Durable consumers {service}-{event}, MaxDeliver=5.
Payloads: UserRegistered(UserId,Username); UserDeleted(UserId); HabitCreated(UserId,HabitId,Name); HabitCompleted(UserId,HabitId,Date,Consistency,Timezone?,DisplayName?,HabitName?,CompletionKind); HabitArchived(UserId,HabitId); FriendRequestSent(From,To); FriendRequestAccepted(U1,U2); FriendRemoved(U1,U2); VisibilityChanged(UserId,HabitId,Old,New); ChallengeCreated(Id,From,To,HabitId); ChallengeCompleted(Id,UserId,Reward).
Consumers: SOCIAL: habit.created->insert default VisibilitySetting (idempotent); habit.archived->delete setting; user.deleted->delete witness links+habits, prefs, visibility, friendships. CHALLENGE: habit.completed->for active challenges where user=recipient & habit matches: dedupe via ProcessedCompletionDates, capture baseline, compute progress, on milestone set Completed + publish challenge.completed; user.deleted->delete all as creator|recipient. NOTIFICATION: habit.completed->resolve friends, filter FriendActivity setting, idempotent notification per friend + push fan-out; friend.request.sent->notify recipient; friend.request.accepted->notify both; challenge.created->notify recipient; challenge.completed->notify user (body=reward); user.deleted->delete notifs/settings/tokens. ACTIVITY: user.registered/habit.created/habit.completed/challenge.*->feed entries; friend.request.accepted->TWO entries; visibility.changed->narrowing soft-deletes habit's entries, widening restores; friend.removed->soft-delete the pair's accepted entries; user.deleted->hard-delete actor + JSONB-referencing entries.

== FLAME / CONSISTENCY (highest-risk port) ==
File: services/habit-service/src/Services/ConsistencyCalculator.cs (static). WindowDays=60, FullWeight=1.0, MinimumWeight=0.5.
Calculate(habit, completions map LocalDate->Kind, today, habitCreatedLocalDate?): (1) windowStart = today-59 inclusive. (2) BACKFILL RULE (commit a35bd06): firstCompletionInWindow = min completion date in [windowStart,today] else habitCreatedDate; scoreStart = min(firstCompletionInWindow, habitCreatedDate); effectiveStart = max(windowStart, scoreStart); return 0 if effectiveStart > today. (3) Daily/Custom: iterate effectiveStart..today, count applicable days (Daily=all; Custom=CustomDays contains weekday), sum GetWeight(kind) for completed; result = Round(sum/applicable*100, 1). (4) Weekly: ISO weeks (Monday start), each overlapping week counts once, weight = best kind in week (Full short-circuits); Round(sum/totalWeeks*100, 1).
CalculateForDateRange: same weighting over arbitrary range clamped to creation date (used by challenges).
Flame levels, asymmetric hysteresis: rising thresholds None<10<=Ember<30<=Steady<55<=Strong<80<=Blazing; falling (only when new<previous) 5/20/40/65, capped at previous. Most call sites pass NO previous level (nothing persisted) -> hysteresis largely dormant; computed fresh per request. No caching anywhere.
TIMEZONE CONTRACT (critical): Completion.LocalDate is computed at WRITE time from the client IANA tz. At read time: owner surfaces (GET /stats, promise evaluation) use the owner's X-Timezone header; SHARE surfaces (/habits/public/**, flame.svg, old /habits/user/{userId}) hardcode UTC (documented must-match contract); challenge range uses ?tz= falling back to UTC. IANA ids validated; invalid -> 400.

== AUTH ==
Password: Argon2id (Konscious): parallelism=1, memory=65536 KiB (64MB), iterations=3, salt 16B random, hash 32B. Stored as "base64(salt):base64(hash)" in one column. Verify = split on ':', recompute, constant-time compare. Go equivalent: x/crypto/argon2.IDKey with identical params + format.
JWT: HS256 symmetric (Jwt:Secret, validated at startup: >=32 chars, not a known placeholder). Claims: sub=userId, email, jti=guid. NO iss/aud (validation disabled). Access lifetime Jwt:AccessTokenMinutes (default 15), ClockSkew=0. Only the gateway validates today.
Refresh: opaque 64 random bytes base64, stored in DB, unique; lifetime Jwt:RefreshTokenDays (default 7); SINGLE-USE rotation on /auth/refresh (old revoked, new issued); all revoked on password change; revoked on logout. Web clients (detected via Sec-Fetch-Site header): refresh token delivered ONLY as HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite=Strict cookie "refresh_token" Path=/auth, and AuthResponse.refreshToken is null. Native: token in JSON body.

== NOTIFICATIONS ==
WebPush NuGet (VAPID, RFC8291/8188). Config WebPush:Subject (mailto:hello@winzy.ai), WebPush:PublicKey, WebPush:PrivateKey - NOT set in compose; live values only in Railway. Missing keys -> skip delivery, keep tokens. DeviceToken.Token = full subscription JSON {endpoint,keys:{p256dh,auth}}. Delivery best-effort: 404/410 Gone -> delete token; transient/429 -> keep. Fan-out on habit.completed: friends via social internal, dedupe+self-filter, FriendActivity filter, idempotency key "habit_completed:{friend}:{actor}:{habit}:{date}", batch-insert notifications, parallel push bounded by semaphore(10), PushDelivered flag set after; duplicate events retry push only when PushDelivered=false.

== CONFIG SURFACE (current) ==
All services: ConnectionStrings__DefaultConnection, Nats__Url, Seq__Url. Gateway: Jwt__Secret, RateLimiting__AuthPermitLimit, ReverseProxy cluster addresses x6, ReverseProxy__TrustedProxies, Cors__Origins (default http://localhost:8081). Auth: Jwt__Secret/AccessTokenMinutes/RefreshTokenDays + 5 service URLs. Notification: WebPush__Subject/PublicKey/PrivateKey. Infra: nats:2-alpine --jetstream, postgres:17-alpine x6 (host 5433-5438), Seq dev-only, gateway 5050:5000, services expose-only 5001-5006.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
GROUND TRUTH 2/2 - FRONTEND INTEGRATION INVENTORY (deep-explore, 2026-07-09). The frontend does NOT change in this rewrite except inside frontend/src/api/*; these are the contracts the Go backend must honor.

== API CLIENT ==
Single hand-rolled fetch wrapper frontend/src/api/client.ts exporting apiRequest<T> + api.{get,post,put,patch,delete}. ~13 domain modules, ~53 wrapper functions, all types handwritten with "Keep in sync with: services/.../*.cs" comments (must be repointed to Go sources). Base URL from EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL, resolved at build time: web prod -> empty string = SAME-ORIGIN (the Go binary must serve the SPA and the API on one origin); web dev + native -> http://localhost:5050.
Client behaviors the Go API must stay compatible with: Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>; credentials:"include" on every request; on 401 exactly one /auth/refresh then one retry (single-flight lock across concurrent 401s); retries only 429/502/503/504 on safe methods (max 2, backoff); 15s timeout; 204 -> undefined.
ERROR BODY SHAPES ARE LOAD-BEARING: mapHttpError maps status -> ApiError{status, code, message, validationErrors?}; 409 reads body.error; 422 (or any body with errors) reads body.errors: Record<string,string[]> (ASP.NET ProblemDetails shape). Go must emit 409 {"error": "..."} and 422 {"errors": {...}} or client-facing messages break.

== AUTH ON CLIENT ==
Web: access token memory-only; refresh token never stored client-side (httpOnly cookie only). Native: localStorage. bootstrapSession() on mount always attempts /auth/refresh on web. THE FRONTEND NEVER DECODES THE JWT - identity comes from AuthResponse.user. Contract: {accessToken, refreshToken, user} + refresh cookie. JWT internals are free to change.

== PUSH ==
subscribeToWebPush(): permission -> GET /notifications/vapid-public-key {publicKey} -> pushManager.subscribe -> POST /notifications/devices {platform:"web_push", token: JSON.stringify(subscription), deviceId:"web_<sha256-prefix-of-endpoint>"}. Unsubscribe: DELETE /notifications/devices {deviceId}. Service worker (frontend/public/sw.js) push payload: JSON {title,body,icon,badge,url,tag}; SW fetch bypasses backend prefixes ["/auth/","/habits","/notifications","/social","/challenges","/friends","/health"] - NOTE /activity and /witness are MISSING from that list (pre-existing; same-origin GETs fall into SPA cache-first path; flag when touching SW or those routes).

== E2E (Playwright) ==
Repo-root e2e/, 11 specs (smoke, auth, public-flame, profile, today, habit-crud, habit-detail, habit-templates, create-challenge, friends, notifications). BASE_URL default http://localhost:8081 (Expo web); backend NOT booted by Playwright - assumes gateway on http://localhost:5050, HARDCODED in fixtures/auth.setup.ts (API_URL env), notifications.spec.ts, create-challenge.spec.ts, friends.spec.ts. Test user e2e-user@winzy.test / TestPassword123! (E2E_USER_* env overridable); auth.setup logs in via UI, falls back to POST /auth/register (accepts 201/409), saves storageState. The Go stack must be reachable on :5050 locally when running E2E against it.

== JEST ==
jest-expo, ~190 test files, no MSW. 36 files mock at the DOMAIN-MODULE boundary (jest.mock("../../api/habits")); only api/__tests__/client.test.ts mocks global.fetch (tests retry/refresh/error-mapping internals). CONSEQUENCE: keep frontend/src/api/* exported names/signatures/return types stable and nearly all Jest tests pass untouched; swapping to a generated client with different exports would rewire 36 mock sites. Strategy: change internals only, keep the facade.

== WEB SERVING (prod, same-origin) ==
services/gateway/Dockerfile.railway: stage 1 node:22-alpine runs "npx expo export --platform web" -> frontend/dist; runtime copies dist -> wwwroot (+ frontend/assets -> wwwroot/assets). Gateway: UseDefaultFiles + UseStaticFiles + YARP for API paths + MapFallbackToFile("index.html") for SPA routes like /@username. No explicit cache headers (framework ETag/Last-Modified only). The Go server must replicate: static serve of the Expo dist, API routes take precedence, SPA fallback to index.html.

== FRONTEND CONFIG ==
EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL is the ONLY backend-related frontend config (inlined at expo export time). VAPID public key is fetched at runtime, not built in. No feature flags. E2E-only env: BASE_URL, API_URL, E2E_USER_EMAIL/USERNAME/PASSWORD, CI.

title: Scaffold Go backend service
description:
Create backend/ as the Go module that will replace the .NET services. Scaffolding only - no business logic. Read epic winzy.ai-rdc7 (br show winzy.ai-rdc7, including its two ground-truth comments) for target architecture and the execution protocol before starting.

BACKGROUND / REASONING: this bead establishes every convention the later port beads inherit - module layout, config, DB access, migration tooling, middleware stack, testing shape, CI. Getting these right here means 10 later beads don't relitigate them. Choices are deliberately boring: stdlib over frameworks because coding agents and a solo maintainer benefit from zero magic.

DESIGN DECISIONS ALREADY MADE (do not reopen):
- stdlib net/http with Go 1.22+ method routing; no web framework. Add chi ONLY if a concrete middleware need demands it (document if so).
- pgx v5 pool for Postgres; sqlc optional where it genuinely helps. ONE database.
- slog JSON logging to stdout (no Seq in the new world; Railway captures stdout).
- Package layout mirrors the future modules: cmd/api/main.go; internal/config, internal/httpserver (router, middleware), internal/db (pool, migrations); feature packages internal/auth, internal/habits, ... arrive in later beads.
- Middleware stack (order): panic recovery -> request logging -> CORS (allowed origin config, default http://localhost:8081 for Expo dev - matches current gateway Cors__Origins). JWT middleware arrives in winzy.ai-rdc7.2; rate limiting arrives with auth.
- Local dev port: 8080 (the old stack still owns 5050 until switchover; beads 8/10 handle the 5050 handover). PORT env-configurable.
- New dedicated compose Postgres service + volume for the Go stack. Do NOT touch the six existing per-service databases or anything under services/.

EXECUTION STEPS (in order - do not skip verification)
1. Read CLAUDE.md, this bead, and br show winzy.ai-rdc7 with comments.
2. Verify current library APIs before writing code (Context7 or official docs): latest stable Go, pgx v5, and the migrations tool - golang-migrate unless you find a concrete blocker; record choice + reasoning in your report. Do NOT guess API signatures.
3. Create backend/ go.mod (module path matching the repo, e.g. github.com/Gabko14/winzy/backend) + the layout above.
4. internal/config: read PORT, DATABASE_URL, LOG_LEVEL, CORS_ORIGIN from env with local-dev defaults; fail fast with a clear error on invalid values.
5. HTTP server with graceful shutdown (SIGTERM/SIGINT) + the middleware stack above.
6. pgx pool + migrations wiring with an initial (empty or minimal) migration; compose service "winzy-db" (postgres:17-alpine, own volume, host port not colliding with 5433-5438).
7. GET /health returning JSON {status, db} with a real DB ping (matches the health convention in CLAUDE.md).
8. Tests per the CLAUDE.md testing rule (happy/edge/error): config parsing (valid, missing, garbage), health handler via httptest (DB up, DB down).
9. Dockerfile: multi-stage, statically linked binary, distroless or scratch final stage.
10. CI: .github/workflows/ci-go.yml on backend/** changes - gofmt check, go vet, go build, go test.
11. backend/README.md: run / test / migrate commands.
12. VERIFY (paste outputs): gofmt -l . empty; go vet ./... clean; go test ./... green; docker build succeeds; with winzy-db up, go run ./cmd/api starts and curl /health returns healthy.
13. STOP. Do not commit, do not push, do not close this bead. Report: files created, decisions (module path, migrations tool), full step-12 output, deviations, open questions. The PM reviews and closes.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- GET /health returns status + real DB connectivity
- Middleware stack: panic recovery, request logging, CORS
- Multi-stage Dockerfile, small static-binary image
- CI workflow: gofmt, vet, build, test
- Dedicated compose Postgres; existing services untouched
- backend/README.md covers run/test/migrate
close_reason: Scaffold implemented (worker-rdc7-1/Sonnet), PM-reviewed and verified: layout, config, middleware stack + logging convention, pgx+golang-migrate, dbtest integration recipe, Dockerfile (distroless), ci-go.yml with -race and Postgres service container. See PM REVIEW comment.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
PM REVIEW ADDENDUM (2026-07-09) - two CONVENTIONS defined here that every later bead inherits; implement both in this bead:

1. LOGGING CONVENTION (the rewrite must be debuggable in production from day one):
- Request-ID middleware: uuid per request; every slog line carries request_id, method, path, status, duration_ms, and user_id when authenticated.
- Hook-event logging: every event emitted through the registry logs event type + key fields at debug level; handler failures log at error with the event payload.
- Startup logs the resolved config with secrets redacted.
2. INTEGRATION-TEST CONVENTION: choose and document the recipe for handler tests against a REAL Postgres (testcontainers-go vs compose winzy-db + TEST_DATABASE_URL - pick one, justify in your report; note the existing pre-push hook already assumes Docker). Provide a reusable test helper (spin up/point at DB, run migrations, truncate between tests). Every module bead's integration tests use this helper.
- CI runs go test -race -v (race detector on; verbose output so CI logs are diagnostic).

ADDITIONAL ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: request-ID present in log output (asserted by test); sample integration test for /health using the documented recipe; -race in CI workflow.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
PM REVIEW (2026-07-09, worker: worker-rdc7-1 on Sonnet): APPROVED. Independently re-ran all gates (gofmt/vet/build/test -race clean; live integration test against compose winzy-db passed; /health verified from the built distroless image by the worker). Two trivia fixes applied by PM directly: dbtest doc comment said "fails fast" where behavior is (correctly) skip; CORS now sets Vary: Origin unconditionally.
Worker's open questions, answered: (1) DB name "winzy" + host port 5439 approved. (2) No stub hook registry in this bead - defining it fresh in rdc7.2 was the correct scoping call. (3) Docker leftovers (winzy-backend-scaffold-test container + winzy-backend:scaffold image) need manual cleanup by the user - the dcg guard blocks container/image removal commands for all agents.
Highlight for the record: worker caught that immutable context.WithValue cannot propagate user_id from an inner auth middleware to the outer request logger, and built the mutable requestState mechanism (internal/httpserver/context.go) with tests - rdc7.2's auth middleware MUST use httpserver.SetUserID, not its own WithValue.

title: Railway cutover to Go service
description:
Cutover runbook: put the Go service live on Railway and retire the old topology. Claude operates Railway via CLI (railway up); deploys are manual, project winzy-staging. BLOCKED BY winzy.ai-rdc7.9 (migration rehearsed) AND winzy.ai-rdc7.12 (green parity run) - both are hard preconditions.

KEY DECISION (2026-07-09): REPURPOSE the existing api-gateway Railway service to run the Go image instead of creating a new service. Why: the Railway-generated public domain (api-gateway-production-9faf.up.railway.app) is bound to the service - reusing the service keeps the public URL, shared flame links, and any installed PWAs working with zero client-side change. Rollback = redeploy the old gateway image to the same service.

ENV CONTINUITY (values copied, not regenerated - this is what makes cutover invisible to users):
- JWT secret: SAME value as today (>=32 chars requirement unchanged)
- VAPID Subject/PublicKey/PrivateKey: SAME values (push subscriptions survive; see winzy.ai-rdc7.6)
- New: DATABASE_URL -> the new single Postgres service
Access tokens are 15-min lived, refresh tokens are not migrated -> users see one re-login; nothing else changes.

RUNBOOK (elaborated into EXECUTION STEPS at dispatch)
1. Create the new Postgres service + volume in winzy-staging (railway CLI); run schema migrations.
2. Pre-cutover: fresh pg_dump of all six old DBs (rollback archive, per winzy.ai-rdc7.9).
3. Announce/accept a short write-freeze (solo app - trivial); run the migration against the new Railway Postgres; run the verification report + owner spot-check.
4. Set env vars on the api-gateway service (JWT/VAPID copied, DATABASE_URL new); railway up the Go Dockerfile.railway to that service.
5. SMOKE VERIFICATION (paste results): /health; register a throwaway user + login; existing user login (proves hash chain in prod); log a completion and check flame math vs pre-cutover value; public flame page + flame.svg; witness link; challenge progress tick; push notification round-trip to a real subscribed browser; activity feed renders.
6. ROLLBACK PATH (documented before step 4): redeploy old gateway image to the service; old internal services + DBs are still running untouched, so rollback is minutes. Define the trigger criteria (any smoke failure not fixable in <1h).
7. STABILITY WINDOW: old services keep running (idle) for ~1 week. Then decommission the 12 old services + their volumes (keep the final dumps archived) - deleting things is the irreversible step; PM confirms with the user before it.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Public URL unchanged and serving the SPA + API from the Go binary
- All smoke checks green in production, incl. existing-user login and push delivery
- Rollback documented and old stack intact until the stability window ends
- Decommission executed only after explicit user confirmation, dumps archived

(EXECUTION STEPS added by the PM at dispatch.)
close_reason: Cutover executed 2026-07-13: Go service live on the unchanged public URL, serving SPA+API from one binary against the new single Railway Postgres 18.4 (winzy_prod). Migration verified exact (14 tables, zero orphans, auth chain audited). Full smoke green including owner's real login vs pre-cutover screenshots, challenge tick, push pipeline to the push-service door, XFF spoof + 429 checks. Rollback documented (dashboard rollback to prior deployment + old stack untouched); rollback archive 2026-07-13_1552. Teardown is rdc7.11.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: PM REVIEW ADDENDUM (2026-07-09): CLIENT-IP BEHIND RAILWAY (picks up the deferred note in rdc7.2 - review found it was never landed anywhere): Railway's edge proxy terminates TLS and sets X-Forwarded-For. The Go rate limiter must key on the real client IP: trust XFF only from the proxy (rightmost-untrusted strategy or Railway-documented header), NEVER trust a client-supplied XFF directly. Verify in smoke: a request with a spoofed X-Forwarded-For from outside must not bypass or poison rate limiting. Add to smoke checklist: GET /auth/export returns a complete document in production; rate-limit headers/429 behavior on the auth endpoints.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: RUNBOOK ADDITION (PM, 2026-07-09): any use of the old stack - rehearsals, parity runs, fixture capture - MUST start with docker compose up -d --build. The parity worker found the running images were 4 months stale (built 2026-03-25) while source was current; unrebuilt images silently test an ancient version.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: CUTOVER PRECONDITION MET (2026-07-13): golden-master zero mismatches on the real migrated dataset (rdc7.12 closed). Rehearsal stack validated end-to-end incl. owner login with pre-migration password. RUNBOOK NOTES accumulating: (1) Railway prod Postgres is 18.4 — use pg18 clients for any dump/restore; (2) TRUSTED_PROXY=true env on the Go service; (3) VAPID_* env from old WebPush__* values (browsers keep push subscriptions); (4) the web bundle MUST be built fresh in the image (Dockerfile.railway does this) — never serve a prebuilt dist folder (stale-dist incident 2026-07-13); (5) rollback artifact: winzy-data-archive/2026-07-12_1945 + documented dump commands.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
RUNBOOK v1 — READY FOR JOINT EXECUTION (PM SapphireLion, 2026-07-13). Both hard blockers closed (rdc7.9 migration + owner check; rdc7.12 parity + golden-master zero mismatches). This comment is the executable script; owner must be present (irreversible-step confirmations + final smoke on his devices). Estimated 45-90 min.

PRE-FLIGHT (PM alone, before the owner session)
P1. tools/migrate: confirm the LOAD TARGET is flag-configurable (--target-url). If it is hardcoded to localhost:5440/winzy_rehearsal, make the tiny change + unit test first (worker task, ~30 min).
P2. Verify Dockerfile.railway builds fresh: docker build -f backend/Dockerfile.railway -t winzy-api-cutover . (the image exports the web bundle inside stage 1 — NEVER a prebuilt dist; stale-dist incident 2026-07-13).
P3. Verify railway CLI auth + project link (railway whoami; railway status = winzy-staging/production).
P4. Decide deploy mechanics: railway up deploys the cwd to the LINKED service; dockerfilePath must be set (railway.json/railway.toml \"build\": {\"dockerfilePath\": \"backend/Dockerfile.railway\"} at repo root, or service settings in dashboard). Verify against current Railway docs at execution time — CLI flags drift.

CUTOVER SEQUENCE
1. NEW POSTGRES: railway add (database postgres) in winzy-staging -> note its DATABASE_URL reference. Postgres major will be 18.x (matches dumps).
2. FRESH DUMPS (rollback archive v2): rerun ~/work/side-projects/winzy-data-archive/dump-railway.sh (uses postgres:18-alpine client; writes a new timestamped dir + row counts). THIS is the freeze point — do it at a quiet hour; ~5 users, no formal freeze needed, but owner confirms nobody is actively logging.
3. MIGRATE INTO RAILWAY PG: restore the fresh dumps into winzy-mig-db sources (tools/migrate restore step), then load with --target-url=<railway DATABASE_PUBLIC_URL>. Emit + review the verification report: all counts exact, zero orphans (compare against the fresh source-row-counts.txt, NOT the July-12 one).
4. ENV ON api-gateway SERVICE (values COPIED from existing services — continuity is the whole trick):
   | new var | source |
   | JWT_SECRET | old gateway Jwt__Secret (same value!) |
   | VAPID_SUBJECT / VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY / VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY | notification-service WebPush__Subject/PublicKey/PrivateKey |
   | DATABASE_URL | the new Postgres service reference |
   | TRUSTED_PROXY | true (Railway edge sets X-Forwarded-For; rate limiter keys real client IP) |
   | CORS_ORIGIN | https://<public domain> (same-origin serving makes this near-moot; set it correctly anyway) |
   | LOG_LEVEL | info |
   (PORT: Railway injects it; the Go binary reads PORT. RATE_LIMIT_* stay at code defaults = prod values.)
5. DEPLOY: railway up onto the api-gateway service (KEY DECISION: reuse the service — public domain api-gateway-production-9faf.up.railway.app stays, PWAs + shared links keep working). Migrations auto-apply on boot against the new DB (already loaded — golang-migrate no-ops on matching schema_migrations).
6. SMOKE (paste every result on this bead):
   a. GET /health -> healthy/db up
   b. SPA loads at the public URL; /@<owner-username> deep link serves the app
   c. Register throwaway user -> login -> create habit -> complete -> flame renders
   d. OWNER logs in with real password (hash-chain in prod) -> habits + history + flame match pre-cutover values (owner screenshots before cutover for comparison)
   e. Public flame page + flame.svg for the owner
   f. Witness link opens; challenge progress ticks on a completion
   g. PUSH: owner's already-subscribed browser receives a push (VAPID continuity proof) — trigger via a friend completion or test path
   h. GET /auth/export returns the complete multi-section document
   i. XFF SPOOF CHECK: curl with forged X-Forwarded-For from outside -> rate limiting NOT keyed on the forged value (verify via headers/429 behavior; addendum 2026-07-09)
   j. 429 behavior on /auth/* endpoints (10/min limit) sane
7. ROLLBACK (decide trigger BEFORE step 5; default: any smoke failure not fixed in <1h): redeploy the previous api-gateway deployment (Railway dashboard/CLI redeploy of the prior image). Old services + old DBs are UNTOUCHED and still running -> rollback is minutes. New PG service stays for a retry.
8. STABILITY WINDOW: old 12 services idle ~1 week. Decommission (rdc7.11) ONLY after explicit owner confirmation; final dumps stay archived forever.

KNOWN FACTS BAKED IN: Railway prod PG=18.4 (pg18 clients only); refresh tokens not migrated -> every user re-logs-in once (the ONLY user-visible change); access tokens 15 min; compose-based old stack must always be booted with --build if ever needed again.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
RUNBOOK v2 DELTAS (PM, 2026-07-13, after a delegated fresh-eyes review — v1 + these deltas = the executable version. Findings were file-verified, not speculative.)

D1 (CRITICAL, replaces pre-flight P1): tools/migrate CANNOT target Railway today — hardcoded target db name, sslmode=disable, and CREATE DATABASE OWNER \"winzy\" from a constant. Fix is bead winzy.ai-z08o (now BLOCKS this bead). The env table's DATABASE_URL for the Go service must then use the ACTUAL target db name decided there.

D2 (HIGH, new checkpoint before step 5): PROVE the deploy target changed. (a) Before railway up: confirm the api-gateway service build setting points at backend/Dockerfile.railway (dashboard or a committed railway config) — no repo railway.json/toml exists today, so this is dashboard state. (b) After deploy: DISCRIMINATOR CHECK, because /health passes on BOTH stacks — curl an API path with no token: the Go binary returns 401 with body {\"error\":\"unauthorized\"}; the old .NET gateway returns an EMPTY 401 body (this exact divergence is parity-allowlisted F2, which makes it a perfect fingerprint). Empty body after deploy = you just redeployed the OLD stack; stop.

D3 (HIGH, smoke 6.i corrected): the rate limiter reads X-Real-IP (only when TRUSTED_PROXY=true) — it NEVER reads X-Forwarded-For. The spoof test must forge X-Real-IP from an untrusted origin and confirm it does not key the limiter. Also VERIFY-LIVE: confirm Railway's edge actually injects X-Real-IP on this plan/region — if it only sets X-Forwarded-For, the Go limiter falls back to socket IP (= Railway's proxy IP for everyone) and rate limits become GLOBAL per-proxy: that must be caught before cutover, not after.

D4 (HIGH, rollback semantics): rollback DISCARDS any writes that landed in the new Postgres after cutover (completions, registrations). Before executing a rollback: pg_dump the new Postgres first so interim writes can be hand-replayed. State this to the owner at the rollback decision point.

D5 (MEDIUM): the write-freeze holds from the step-2 dump THROUGH step 5's traffic swap — not just around the dump. With ~6 users this is minutes of honesty, not process theater; re-verify source row counts right before deploy if the window stretched.

D6 (MEDIUM): VERIFY-LIVE — confirm the Railway service health-checks /health before swapping traffic (build-then-swap vs restart-in-place), and that redeploy-previous works for railway-up deployments (this is the rollback mechanism!). Also sweep the service's existing env for .NET-era leftovers (ASPNETCORE_*, explicit PORT) and delete them.

D7 (MEDIUM, smoke addition 6.k): open the OWNER'S INSTALLED PWA (home-screen icon, standalone mode — not a fresh tab) and confirm it serves the new build after the service-worker update cycle; sw.js is cache-first for hashed assets + network-first for navigations, so it should self-heal, but the installed app is the slowest path and the one real users live in.

D8 (LOW): step-2 fresh dumps go to a new timestamped dir under ~/work/side-projects/winzy-data-archive/ — record the exact path on this bead for rdc7.11's keep-list.

VERIFY-LIVE CHECKLIST (day-of, before step 1): dashboard Dockerfile path; healthcheck config + swap behavior; redeploy-previous availability; Railway PG default db/role names + sslmode=require on the public proxy; X-Real-IP injection; leftover env vars; current railway CLI syntax for all of the above.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: VERIFY-LIVE RESULTS (PM, 2026-07-13, pre-cutover): (1) Deploy mechanism: old gateway used RAILWAY_DOCKERFILE_PATH=services/gateway/Dockerfile.railway env var; replaced by committed railway.json (build.dockerfilePath=backend/Dockerfile.railway + deploy.healthcheckPath=/health, timeout 120s) — config-as-code documented to override dashboard; delete the env var at the env step to remove ambiguity. (2) Healthcheck: NO service has healthcheckPath today; with railway.json the Go deploy is build-then-swap gated on /health 200 (zero-downtime per Railway docs). (3) Rollback: canRedeploy=true on gateway deployment; redeploy of OLDER deployments is DASHBOARD-ONLY (three-dots Rollback/Redeploy on a previous deployment; CLI railway redeploy only redoes the latest). (4) X-Real-IP: Railway staff (2026-03) — X-Real-IP is BROKEN behind their CDN (set to CDN edge IP, acknowledged bug); contract is leftmost X-Forwarded-For (edge owns the header, strips client-supplied values). FIXED in code (commit 2d9eef8): ClientIP now reads leftmost XFF under TRUSTED_PROXY; smoke 6.i becomes a black-box check — 11 rapid /auth/login attempts with ROTATING forged X-Forwarded-For values must still 429 (if spoofing worked each request would get a fresh bucket). (6) VAPID: WebPush__Subject/PublicKey/PrivateKey confirmed present on notification-service. (7) Railway default Postgres is 16 (ghcr.io/railwayapp-templates/postgres-ssl:latest) — dumps are PG 18.4, so the new DB service MUST be pinned to the 18 template/tag; verify SELECT version() before load. (8) CLI v4.23.0: railway variables --set 'K=v' --service X --skip-deploys; cross-ref ${{Postgres.DATABASE_PUBLIC_URL}}. (9) Cache headers 7w6i landed (97857e0) — ships in first Go deploy.

title: Teardown: remove .NET stack from repo
description:
Repo teardown after cutover is stable: remove the .NET world and make the repo tell the truth about the Go architecture. Runs ONLY after the winzy.ai-rdc7.10 stability window closes and the old Railway services are decommissioned.

DELETE: services/ (all 7 .NET services incl. gateway + both Dockerfile.railway files), shared/ (Winzy.Contracts, Winzy.Common, Winzy.Testing.Common), Winzy.slnx, NATS + six postgres + Seq entries in docker-compose (compose becomes: backend, winzy-db, and whatever the frontend dev flow needs), .github/workflows/ci-backend.yml (the .NET one; ci-go.yml from winzy.ai-rdc7.1 remains), .NET portions of the husky pre-push hook (this is the hook that made pushes slow - after this bead, pushes get fast, which the owner will enjoy).

REWRITE: CLAUDE.md - the rewrite banner goes away; "Key Design Decisions" is rewritten for the Go system (single service + modules, hook registry semantics, per-surface timezone contract, error body shapes 409/422, port + same-origin serving, public endpoint allowlist, Argon2id format, VAPID continuity, "no caching in flame math" as a deliberate choice). README - stack description, local dev (docker compose up: Go + Postgres; npm run web), test commands, deploy note (railway up, manual). Check .beads content? No - beads are history, leave them.

ALSO SWEEP: root .gitignore .NET entries, e2e docs that mention the old stack, any lingering "Keep in sync with .cs" comments (should be gone after winzy.ai-rdc7.8.1 - verify), Seq references.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Zero C#/NATS/Seq references in code, compose, CI, hooks, or docs (grep proves it)
- docker compose up gives a working local stack; E2E suite still green against it
- CLAUDE.md Key Design Decisions describe only the Go system; README accurate
- Pre-push hook exercises only Go + frontend gates and is measurably fast
- git history preserves everything (no history rewrite - deletion is just a commit)

(EXECUTION STEPS added by the PM at dispatch. P2: valuable but nothing breaks if it waits a week.)

title: Port auth module to Go
description:
Port the auth module to Go: users, register/login/refresh/logout, profile, password change, account deletion, data export, user search - plus the JWT middleware and the in-process event hook registry that every later module builds on. Ground truth: epic comment 1 sections ENDPOINTS/AUTH; frontend contract in epic comment 2.

BACKGROUND / REASONING: auth goes first after scaffolding because (a) every other endpoint needs the JWT middleware and user identity, (b) the hook registry (the NATS replacement) is defined here and consumed by every later module, and (c) hash/token compatibility is the highest-consequence correctness constraint in the whole rewrite - existing users must keep logging in after migration.

COMPATIBILITY FACTS (from the C# source - verbatim, do not approximate):
- Password hashing: Argon2id, parallelism=1, memory=65536 KiB, iterations=3, salt=16 random bytes, hash=32 bytes; stored as "base64(salt):base64(hash)" in one column. Go: x/crypto/argon2.IDKey with identical params; verify = split ':', recompute, constant-time compare. Migrated hashes must verify unchanged - write a test with a known .NET-produced hash string.
- JWT: HS256 symmetric secret (>=32 chars, validate at startup and reject known placeholders); claims sub=userId, email, jti=uuid; NO iss/aud; access lifetime 15 min default (env), clock skew 0. The frontend never decodes the JWT, so internals beyond this are free.
- Refresh tokens: opaque 64 random bytes base64, DB-stored, unique, 7-day default, SINGLE-USE rotation on /auth/refresh (revoke old, issue new); revoke all on password change; revoke on logout. Web clients (detect via Sec-Fetch-Site header presence, as the C# does): refresh token ONLY in HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite=Strict cookie "refresh_token" Path=/auth, and response refreshToken=null. Native: token in JSON body.
- Response contract: {accessToken, refreshToken, user} where user = UserProfile. Error shapes: 409 {"error": "..."}, 422 {"errors": {field: [messages]}} - load-bearing for the frontend error mapper.
- Rate limiting moves from the gateway: /auth/* endpoints 10/min/IP (env-overridable; compose dev uses 1000), general 300/min/IP. Client-IP from the socket is fine for now (Railway proxy nuance is revisited at cutover).

SCOPE / TASKS
1. users + refresh_tokens tables (see epic comment 1 data model; Email/Username unique lowercased).
2. Endpoints: POST /auth/register|login|refresh (public), POST /auth/logout, GET+PUT /auth/profile, PUT /auth/password (revokes all refresh tokens), DELETE /auth/account, GET /auth/export (rate 1/60s/user; now a single-DB query assembled per module - stub non-auth sections until those modules land), GET /auth/users/search?q= (min 2 chars, cap 20).
3. JWT middleware: validates Bearer token, puts userId into request context (the in-process replacement for the gateway X-User-Id contract). Public-route allowlist per the epic.
4. EVENT HOOK REGISTRY (the NATS replacement, used by every later module): a small in-process pub/sub - modules register handlers for typed events (UserRegistered, UserDeleted, HabitCreated, HabitCompleted, HabitArchived, FriendRequestSent/Accepted, FriendRemoved, VisibilityChanged, ChallengeCreated/Completed - payloads in epic comment 1). Handlers run synchronously in the caller's request; document the transactional stance (same-tx where the module shares the DB - which is now always). Auth emits UserRegistered and UserDeleted.
5. DELETE /auth/account: emits UserDeleted through the registry (cascades arrive as modules land), then deletes auth-owned rows - all within one transaction where possible. The old system published-then-deleted for durability; in-process we get atomicity instead.
6. Old /auth/internal/* endpoints are NOT ported - resolve-username and batch-profiles become exported Go functions for other modules.

CONSIDERATIONS
- Test hash compatibility against a REAL hash exported from the current auth_db (grab one for the e2e test user, not a personal account).
- Keep JWT secret env name simple (JWT_SECRET); at cutover the CURRENT secret value is reused so nothing else changes.
- Sec-Fetch-Site detection: all modern browsers send it; document the fallback (no header = native = token in body), matching current behavior.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A hash string produced by the .NET Argon2id implementation verifies in Go (test with captured fixture)
- Register/login/refresh/logout round-trip incl. cookie behavior for web and body behavior for native (integration tests)
- Single-use refresh rotation proven by test (reusing an old token fails)
- Password change revokes all refresh tokens (test)
- JWT middleware rejects missing/expired/garbage tokens; public allowlist matches the epic exactly
- Hook registry with typed events + tests; UserRegistered/UserDeleted emitted
- 409/422 error body shapes verified by test
- Auth endpoints rate-limited; limits env-configurable
- Full happy/edge/error coverage per CLAUDE.md

(EXECUTION STEPS added by the PM at dispatch.)
close_reason: Auth module ported (worker-rdc7-2/Sonnet), PM-reviewed and verified: Argon2id compat proven against a real .NET hash fixture, JWT HS256 parity incl. placeholder rejection, single-use refresh rotation (FOR UPDATE), web-cookie vs native-body flows, hook + export registries, rate limiting, ~105 integration tests. Validation errors are 400 (real C# behavior; ground truth corrected). See PM REVIEW comment.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
PM REVIEW ADDENDUM (2026-07-09):
1. EXPORT-SECTION REGISTRY (closes a feature gap found in review - the old GET /auth/export fans out to five services' /internal/export; without this, data export would ship incomplete): alongside the event hook registry, define an export registry - each module registers func(userId) -> (sectionName, JSON). GET /auth/export assembles auth's own section + all registered sections into one document. Match the C# export content exactly (check what auth exports - profile data, never the password hash). Rate limit 1/60s/user preserved. Later module beads register their sections (addenda on 3.3, 4, 5, 6, 7).
2. Login updates User.LastLoginAt (in the data model; easy to drop silently - test it).
3. Logging per the rdc7.1 convention, plus auth-specific events: login success/failure (never log passwords or tokens), refresh rotation, revocations, account deletion.
4. Integration tests use the rdc7.1 recipe.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
DISPATCH: EXECUTION STEPS (PM, 2026-07-09 - follow in order, do not skip verification)
1. CONTEXT: read CLAUDE.md ("PM / Worker Split" binds you); br show winzy.ai-rdc7.2 + br comments list winzy.ai-rdc7.2 (incl. the PM REVIEW ADDENDUM: export registry, LastLoginAt, logging); br show winzy.ai-rdc7 + its comments (ground truth); br comments list winzy.ai-rdc7.1 (PM REVIEW - especially: your auth middleware MUST publish the user id via httpserver.SetUserID from the scaffold, NOT your own context.WithValue, or request logs lose user_id).
2. Read the scaffold you are building on: backend/README.md, internal/httpserver (middleware order, SetUserID), internal/config, internal/db, internal/dbtest, migrations/. Follow its conventions exactly.
3. Read the C# spec: services/auth-service/src - PasswordHasher.cs, TokenService.cs (incl. the JWT secret placeholder list - port it), endpoint handlers, refresh-token logic, request validation rules, and the auth-service test suite. The C# tests are your porting spec.
4. Verify library APIs before coding (Context7/official docs): golang.org/x/crypto/argon2 (IDKey) and your JWT library choice (golang-jwt/jwt/v5 is the default candidate - justify whatever you pick). No other new deps without written justification.

CAPTURE A REAL HASH FIXTURE: docker compose up -d nats auth-db auth-service, register a throwaway user against the OLD stack (POST http://localhost:5001/auth/register directly, or via gateway :5050 with the full stack), then read its password_hash from auth_db (psql on localhost:5433). Save the plaintext+hash pair as a test fixture. Your Go verifier MUST accept it. Do not use any real person's account.
6. Migration 0002: users + refresh_tokens tables per the epic data-model comment (unique lowercased email/username, token unique, cascade).
7. internal/events: the typed hook registry per the bead description (all 11 event types from the epic comment; synchronous dispatch; document transactional stance; hook-event logging per the rdc7.1 convention). internal/export: the export-section registry from the addendum.
8. Argon2id hasher (exact params + salt:hash base64 format) and token service (HS256, sub/email/jti, 15min default, secret validated at startup incl. ported placeholder rejection).
9. Refresh flow: opaque 64B tokens, single-use rotation, 7d default, revoke-all on password change, revoke on logout; web-vs-native via Sec-Fetch-Site; httpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookie refresh_token Path=/auth for web (refreshToken null in body), body for native.
10. Endpoints + JWT middleware + rate limiting (auth 10/min/IP, general 300/min, env-overridable, in-memory limiter is fine single-instance) + public allowlist per epic. Error shapes 409 {"error"} / 422 {"errors"}. Emit UserRegistered/UserDeleted; DELETE account in one transaction; GET /auth/export assembles registered sections (auth section real, others appear as modules land); LastLoginAt updated on login.
11. Tests: unit + integration (dbtest recipe) per CLAUDE.md happy/edge/error, incl.: .NET hash fixture verifies; rotation single-use (reuse fails); password change revokes all; cookie vs body behavior both ways; middleware rejects bad/expired tokens; allowlist exact; rate limiter 429s; 409/422 shapes.
12. VERIFY (paste outputs): gofmt -l empty; go vet -tags=integration ./...; go build; go test -race ./...; go test -tags=integration -race -v ./... against winzy-db; manual curl round-trip register->login->refresh->refresh-reuse-fails->logout with cookie jar (paste the session).
13. STOP. No commit, no push, no bead changes. Report: files, decisions (JWT lib, limiter design, registry API shape), full step-12 output, deviations, open questions.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
PM REVIEW (2026-07-09, worker: worker-rdc7-2 on Sonnet): APPROVED. Independently re-ran all gates (unit + full integration suite against winzy-db, ~105 integration tests in internal/auth); read password.go, token.go, middleware.go, store.go rotation, migration 0002, main.go. The .NET hash fixture is genuine (captured from the live auth-service, capture procedure documented in the test) with positive + negative cases. One trivia fix by PM (stale test-filename reference in password.go).

DECISIONS on the worker's open questions:
1. 400-not-422 for validation errors: CONFIRMED. The worker verified actual C# behavior three ways (Results.ValidationProblem default, C# test assertions, frontend mapHttpError accepting "errors" at any status). The epic ground-truth note saying 422 was wrong; corrections filed on the epic and bead 12. Real behavior beats my spec - exactly the right call.
2. sqlc vs hand-rolled SQL: CONVENTION SET - hand-rolled SQL everywhere, queries next to their callers. Consistency beats codegen at this scale; revisit only if the habits module's query surface becomes genuinely painful.
3. Register/Login non-transactional (matching C#'s actual behavior): approved as-is.
4. Compose containers used for fixture capture (nats/auth-db/auth-service) are normal dev-stack artifacts, stopped - no cleanup debt beyond rdc7.1's leftover test container/image.

FORWARD NOTES for later beads (also apply at dispatch):
- The public-route allowlist is EXACT-MATCH "METHOD path". Beads 3.3 (GET /habits/public/**, flame.svg) and 4 (GET /social/witness/{token}) need prefix/pattern support added to auth.Middleware when they land - plan for it, do not bolt on per-route hacks.
- Anything mounted on the top-level mux outside /auth needs its allowlist entry in cmd/api/main.go (the /health lesson - worker caught it via live curl; no automated test covers main.go wiring yet).
- Auth middleware correctly uses httpserver.SetUserID; integration test harness goes through httpserver.New so the wiring is exercised identically to production.

title: Port habits, completions, and Flame to Go
description:
COORDINATING PARENT for the habit-service port - the heart of the product. Split into three sub-beads because the work is genuinely separable and the middle piece (the flame engine) is the single highest-risk port in the rewrite:

- winzy.ai-rdc7.3.1 - schema, habit CRUD, completions (incl. Honest Minimums data model)
- winzy.ai-rdc7.3.2 - the flame/consistency engine (port ConsistencyCalculator exactly; golden tests)
- winzy.ai-rdc7.3.3 - Flame Promises + the public flame endpoints (page + flame.svg)

Chain: 3.1 -> 3.2 -> 3.3. This parent closes when all three are verified. Ground truth for all three: epic comment 1 (data model, endpoints, FLAME section). The C# habit-service source and its tests are the spec; port tests alongside code.

WHY THIS ORDER: completions data semantics (LocalDate written in the client's timezone, CompletionKind weighting) must exist before the calculator that consumes them; promises and public pages consume the calculator.
close_reason: All three children verified and closed: 3.1 habits/completions CRUD, 3.2 flame engine (golden-verified exact port), 3.3 promises + public flame surfaces. The habits module port is complete: CRUD, completions, consistency engine, stats, promises with lazy resolution, public page + flame.svg, export section, UserDeleted cascade.

title: Port social module to Go
description:
Port social-service to Go: friendships, habit visibility, social preferences, and Witness Links. Ground truth: epic winzy.ai-rdc7 comment 1 (ENDPOINTS/SOCIAL, DATA MODEL social_db, NATS consumers for social). C# source services/social-service/src is the spec.

BACKGROUND: this module is the "passive accountability" layer - who can see whose Flame. It was the heaviest NATS consumer; in the monolith all of that becomes hook handlers and direct function calls, which removes an entire class of eventual-consistency bugs (e.g. a habit existing without a visibility row because a subscriber was down).

DATA MODEL (exact): Friendship(UserId, FriendId, Status Pending/Accepted string; unique(UserId,FriendId); an accepted friendship = TWO rows, one per direction). VisibilitySetting(UserId, HabitId, Visibility Private/Friends/Public string; unique(UserId,HabitId)). SocialPreference(UserId unique, DefaultHabitVisibility default Private). WitnessLink(OwnerId, Token unique = base64url of 32 random bytes, Label?, RevokedAt? null=active). WitnessLinkHabit(composite PK link+habit = per-link habit allowlist).

ENDPOINTS: POST /social/friends/request; PUT /social/friends/request/{id}/accept (creates the reverse row) | /decline; DELETE /social/friends/{friendId} (deletes BOTH directions, emits FriendRemoved); GET /social/friends?page&pageSize (enriched with profiles + current flame - now direct calls into auth and habits packages, formerly internal HTTP); GET /social/friends/requests + /requests/count; GET /social/friends/{friendId}/profile; PUT /social/visibility/{habitId} (validates habit ownership via habits package, emits VisibilityChanged with old+new values); GET /social/visibility (batch); GET+PUT /social/preferences; witness-links CRUD + POST /social/witness-links/{id}/rotate; PUBLIC GET /social/witness/{token}.

HOOK HANDLERS (registry from winzy.ai-rdc7.2, replacing NATS consumers):
- HabitCreated -> insert VisibilitySetting at the user's DefaultHabitVisibility (idempotent - tolerate existing row)
- HabitArchived -> delete the VisibilitySetting
- UserDeleted -> delete witness-link-habits, witness links, preferences, visibility settings, friendships (both directions)

WITNESS LINKS specifics (port faithfully): public endpoint returns noindex header; unknown/revoked token -> constant-time 404 (no oracle for token validity); response covers only the per-link habit allowlist; flame values via the habits package (share-surface UTC contract); rotate = new token, old immediately dead.

EXPORTS FOR OTHER MODULES (replace old /social/internal/*): friendsOf(userId) -> friendIds; areFriends(u1,u2); visibleHabits(ownerId, viewerId) - consumed by challenges (friendship validation), notifications (fan-out), activity (feed filter), habits (public page filter integration point left in 3.3).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Two-row friendship model exact: request->Pending single row; accept->reverse row; remove->both rows gone (tests)
- Default-visibility-on-habit-creation via hook, idempotent (test double-fire)
- Visibility change emits old+new; ownership validated (404/403 per C# behavior)
- Witness links: constant-time 404 verified by test structure (same code path for missing/revoked), noindex header present, allowlist enforced, rotate kills old token
- UserDeleted handler removes every social row for the user (test)
- Wire the 3.3 public-page visibility integration point
- Happy/edge/error coverage per CLAUDE.md; port C# social tests

(EXECUTION STEPS added by the PM at dispatch.)
close_reason: Social module ported and PM-verified: two-row friendships, per-habit visibility + preferences, witness links (constant-time 404, token redaction in logs), three hook handlers under the rdc7.13 tx contract, public-page visibility filter wired into habits, social export section. Two review rounds (PM line-read + gpt-5.6-sol adversarial pass), 16-fix round, all gates green on an exclusive final run.

comments.text: PM REVIEW ADDENDUM (2026-07-09): register the SOCIAL EXPORT SECTION in the export registry from rdc7.2 (friendships, visibility settings, preferences, witness links - match old /social/internal/export/{userId} exactly). Integration tests per the rdc7.1 recipe (two-row friendship invariants tested against real Postgres). Logging per convention; witness-token lookups log outcomes WITHOUT logging the token value.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
EXECUTION STEPS (PM dispatch, 2026-07-11). The bead description above holds the data model, endpoint list, and acceptance criteria - this comment adds ordering, integration facts, and conventions established by beads 3.1-3.3. The C# social-service is the spec; read it before writing Go.

SPEC FILES (read fully first): services/social-service/src/Endpoints/{FriendEndpoints,VisibilityEndpoints,WitnessLinkEndpoints,InternalEndpoints}.cs, src/Entities/*, src/Data/Migrations/* (exact schema), src/Subscribers/* (hook handler semantics, incl. HabitCreatedSubscriber's default-visibility insert), and ALL FOUR test files in services/social-service/tests/ (port every case).

STEP 1 - migration 0005_social.{up,down}.sql mirroring the EF migrations exactly (friendships, visibility_settings, social_preferences, witness_links, witness_link_habits; string enum columns store C# PascalCase values, matching 0003/0004's convention; unique constraints per the bead's DATA MODEL; no FK to users, matching the rest of the schema).

EVERY handler that writes resolves its querier via db.QuerierFrom(ctx, fallback) - the rdc7.13 contract (see internal/events package doc + backend/README "Transactional cascades" + habits.handleUserDeleted as reference). Extend the existing cascade integration tests (rollback + commit atomicity now spanning auth+habits+social rows).

STEP 4 - cross-module reads. social may import internal/auth and internal/habits directly (no cycle in that direction): friend-list enrichment uses auth's exported batch-profile function (see auth service.go - the /auth/internal/batch-profiles replacement) and habits' exported consistency path for current flame values. Visibility change validates habit ownership through an exported habits function - if none exists yet, add a minimal exported lookup to habits rather than duplicating SQL.

STEP 5 - exported visibility API for other modules: friendsOf(userID), areFriends(a,b), and the visibility filter the bead's EXPORTS section lists. Then WIRE THE 3.3 INTEGRATION POINT: internal/habits/promise_public.go's PublicFlameProfile and FlameBadge currently show all non-archived habits (marked INTEGRATION POINT (winzy.ai-rdc7.4)). habits must NOT import social - add a narrow interface + setter on habits.Service (exactly like SetUsernameResolver) that social's public-visibility filter satisfies; wire it in cmd/api/main.go. Semantics from C# PublicEndpoints.FetchVisibility + filtering: defaultIsPublic ? all except explicitly-excluded : only explicitly-visible; viewer=public. Degraded stays hardcoded false (in-process call cannot degrade - keep the comment honest about that). Update the 3.3 public-page tests that assumed everything visible.

STEP 6 - witness links: token = base64url of 32 random bytes; PUBLIC GET /social/witness/{token} (add "GET /social/witness/*" prefix entry to the allowlist in cmd/api/main.go - the isPublicRoute convention from 3.3); constant-time 404 = ONE code path for unknown AND revoked tokens (test asserts same status/body/shape for both); noindex robots header (match C# header name/value verbatim); rotate = new token, old immediately dead; response limited to the per-link habit allowlist; flame values via habits with time.UTC (share-surface contract). NEVER log a token value - log outcomes only (PM ADDENDUM above).

STEP 7 - export section: exportReg.Register("social", s.exportSection) - SINGULAR-name lesson from 3.3: the C# InternalEndpoints.cs literally says service = "social", use exactly that. Match the old /social/internal/export/{userId} payload field-for-field, and return export.ErrNoData (silent skip, no warning) exactly where the C# returns NotFound - read its empty-data condition, don't guess.

STEP 8 - routes + wiring in cmd/api/main.go (social.NewService(pool, registry, exportReg, logger) or equivalent signature, RegisterRoutes, allowlist entry, habits setter). Watch for ServeMux pattern ambiguity like 3.3's habitOrPublicGET - if two GET patterns conflict (literal vs wildcard at different segments), merge into one registration with manual dispatch rather than reordering.

STEP 9 - while you are in dbtest: consolidate the duplicated rowExists test helper (auth + habits cascade tests each have a copy) into internal/dbtest and update both call sites - planned cleanup assigned to this bead.

STEP 10 - tests per CLAUDE.md (happy/edge/error) with the dbtest recipe (//go:build integration, TEST_DATABASE_URL, -p 1). comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
PM REVIEW RECORD (2026-07-11/12): Sonnet worker implemented all 10 EXECUTION STEPS; PM line-read every non-test source file (~2,900 lines across models/store/service/witness/crossmodule/handlers/export/routes + habits-side integration) against FriendEndpoints.cs / VisibilityEndpoints.cs / WitnessLinkEndpoints.cs / InternalEndpoints.cs / Subscribers, verified the migration against both EF migrations column-for-column, and re-ran all gates independently. GPT adversarial pass ran on gpt-5.6-sol (the id that works on the account) and contributed materially.

Fix round 1 (16 fixes + migration one-liner, all verified fixed in the diff, gates green):
- Atomicity vs C# single SaveChangesAsync: accept-friendship (fwd UPDATE + reverse INSERT) now one tx, lookup inside; RemoveFriend now ONE both-directions DELETE with RowsAffected 404; witness create/update each one tx (update's allowlist replace could previously die half-way).
- Parity: witness habitIds now UUID-validated at decode (was Postgres 22P02 -> 500 + ghost link; C# 400s); friends-list flame average now habits.RoundNET(x,1) banker's (was round-half-up; new exported net-compat wrappers RoundNET/UTF16Len in internal/habits/net_compat.go, consistency.go untouched); witness label length in UTF-16 units (was bytes); witness update validation order matches C# (404 before count check); export friends ASC uncapped (was DESC + 1<<20 cap); degraded FriendProfile keeps friendId; BatchProfiles failures degrade to null profiles in ListFriends/ListFriendRequests (matching C# FetchProfileMap) with deterministic incoming-then-outgoing id order; friendId null/"" are decode failures (C# Guid JsonException) while omitted/all-zero stay "FriendId is required"; omitted visibility field defaults to Private (was persisting ""); pageSize<1 clamps to 1 (C# Math.Clamp).
- Hardening: typed conflictError replaces string-prefix parsing (bare-sentinel leak now impossible); witness tokens redacted from BOTH RequestLogging and Recovery panic logs via redactPath + sensitive-prefix threading through httpserver.New (old gateway logged full paths incl. tokens — deliberately stricter here); habit lifecycle events now transactional per rdc7.13 (CreateHabit emits HabitCreated inside its insert tx, ArchiveHabit emits inside its existing tx; failing handler aborts the operation — closes the "habit without a visibility row" eventual-consistency class the bead names; social-origin emits stay post-commit logged-not-failed, consumers are notification-class).
- Migration: dropped two DEFAULT 'Private' clauses EF never had.

GPT (gpt-5.6-sol) contributed: the token-in-request-logs leak, the {} -> ""-visibility persistence bug, the post-commit lifecycle-event gap, pageSize clamp, export cap, migration defaults. PM contributed: the three atomicity gaps, UUID validation, banker's rounding, UTF-16 length, validation order, export order, degraded-response shape, profile degradation policy, typed conflict, friendId null. One GPT finding REJECTED: C#'s bare-string body for the max-50 witness-habit error is a source-side inconsistency; the {"error"} shape deviation stands as documented.

Accepted documented divergences: no /social/internal/* HTTP routes (exported Go methods instead); no 503 branches (in-process calls); missing-X-User-Id tests don't port (JWT middleware 401s first); intQueryParam non-numeric -> default (vs ASP.NET auto-400); witness links added to the export INCLUDING live token (user-visible data via GET /social/witness-links; C# never exported them at all — gap-fill approved); no fault-injection seam for in-process degradation branches (code-reviewed instead); "Invalid visibility value..." message unreachable behind Go's stricter decode.

OPERATIONAL LESSON (cost ~3 contaminated gate runs): two concurrent `go test -tags=integration` INVOCATIONS against the same winzy-db clobber each other (each test truncates all tables) -> nondeterministic vanished/phantom rows and even cascade deadlocks. PM and worker were racing suites. Rule now in backend/README ("Test" section): one integration runner at a time. Neither failure signature was a code bug — final exclusive run: every package green under -race, uncached.

Verified independently: two-row friendship invariants (incl. 5x concurrent-accept test: exactly one 200, rest 404, never 500), constant-time witness 404 (single query shape for unknown/revoked; noindex/no-store headers on 404s too), UserDeleted full-cascade rollback+commit atomicity now spanning auth+habits+social, HabitCreated double-fire idempotency, public-page visibility filtering wired end-to-end (rdc7.3.3's INTEGRATION POINT closed), export section "social" + ErrNoData semantics, rowExists consolidated into dbtest. internal/social: 129 integration subtests; habits grew to 177.

title: Port challenges module to Go
description:
Port challenge-service to Go. Ground truth: epic winzy.ai-rdc7 comment 1 (ENDPOINTS/CHALLENGES, DATA MODEL challenge_db, challenge NATS consumer). C# source services/challenge-service/src is the spec.

SCOPE FACT (verified 2026-07-09): challenge TEMPLATES are frontend-only presets - there is NO backend artifact for them. Nothing template-related to port; the API takes fully-specified MilestoneType + TargetValue.

DATA MODEL (exact): Challenge(HabitId, CreatorId, RecipientId, MilestoneType ConsistencyTarget/DaysInPeriod/TotalCompletions/CustomDateRange/ImprovementMilestone string, TargetValue double, PeriodDays int, RewardDescription, Status Active/Completed/Claimed/Cancelled/Expired string, CurrentProgress double 0-1, EndsAt, CompletedAt?, ClaimedAt?, CompletionCount int, ProcessedCompletionDates JSON set of dates, CustomStartDate?, CustomEndDate?, BaselineConsistency? double; UNIQUE (CreatorId,RecipientId,HabitId) WHERE status='Active').

ENDPOINTS: POST /challenges (creator sets a challenge on a RECIPIENT's habit; validates friendship via the social package - was an internal HTTP call); GET /challenges?page&pageSize&status&since (status is DERIVED for filtering - read the C# logic for how Expired is derived from EndsAt vs stored status); GET /challenges/{id}; PUT /challenges/{id}/claim (only Completed -> Claimed; the recipient/creator rules per C# source); DELETE /challenges/{id} (creator only).

PROGRESS ENGINE (was the habit.completed NATS consumer, becomes a HabitCompleted hook handler): for each Active challenge where the completing user is the recipient and the habit matches: dedupe via ProcessedCompletionDates (a backfilled or re-sent date must not double-count); capture BaselineConsistency on first event for ImprovementMilestone; compute progress per milestone type - ConsistencyTarget uses the event's consistency; DaysInPeriod/TotalCompletions count processed dates; CustomDateRange calls the flame package CalculateForDateRange (was internal HTTP /habits/internal/{id}/consistency, tz param falls back UTC); update CurrentProgress (0-1); on milestone reached set Completed + CompletedAt and emit ChallengeCompleted(reward). READ THE C# SUBSCRIBER CAREFULLY - each milestone type has its own progress formula.

SIMPLIFICATION NOTE: the C# publishes challenge.completed BEFORE saving (a NATS-durability workaround). In-process, emit and save in the SAME transaction - simpler and strictly safer; document this as an intentional improvement, and expect notification/feed side effects to be transactional.

HOOK HANDLERS: HabitCompleted (above); UserDeleted -> delete all challenges where user is creator OR recipient. Emits: ChallengeCreated (on POST), ChallengeCompleted.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All 5 milestone types' progress formulas ported with per-type tests (incl. baseline capture for ImprovementMilestone and clamped date ranges for CustomDateRange)
- ProcessedCompletionDates dedupe proven (same-date replay does not double-count)
- Unique-active constraint behavior on POST matches C# (conflict shape 409 {"error"})
- Derived-status filtering (esp. Expired) matches C#
- Friendship validation on create; claim/delete permission rules per C#
- UserDeleted cleanup test
- Happy/edge/error coverage per CLAUDE.md; port C# challenge tests

(EXECUTION STEPS added by the PM at dispatch.)
close_reason: Challenges module ported: all 5 milestone formulas + progress engine (own-tx HabitCompleted handler), derived-status filtering, unique-active 409, export section, UserDeleted cascade. PM line-read vs C# spec + 4-fix review round, worker+PM integration green under -race. First ntm-managed grok worker dispatch.
