Auth allowlist prefix matching has no bypass (mux 301s unclean paths). Lazy resolution covered incl. exact-EndDate boundary and owner-timezone date-line pair. All 42 C# PromiseEndpointTests cases mapped.

title: OpenAPI spec + typed contract behind the api facade
description:
Give the Go API a machine-readable contract and put it to work in the frontend WITHOUT breaking the facade. Ground truth: epic winzy.ai-rdc7 comment 2 (API CLIENT section - read it fully before deciding anything).

TASKS
1. Produce an OpenAPI spec for the full Go API surface (choose: annotate/generate from Go code, or maintain a hand-written spec verified by contract tests - justify the choice; whichever drifts less for a solo project wins).
2. Generate TypeScript types from the spec (types only is acceptable; a generated low-level client is optional). Wire them into frontend/src/api/* INTERNALS: replace the handwritten request/response interfaces with generated ones (or alias them), while keeping every EXPORTED name, signature, and return type of the domain modules identical - the 36 Jest mock sites and all call sites must not change.
3. Keep client.ts behavior byte-compatible: Bearer header, credentials include, single-flight 401 refresh + one retry, retry 429/502/503/504 on safe methods, 15s timeout, 204 -> undefined, ApiError mapping incl. 409 {"error"} and 422 {"errors"} shapes.
4. Replace every "Keep in sync with: services/.../*.cs" comment with a pointer to the Go source or the OpenAPI spec.
5. Check-in a rerunnable generation script (npm script or make target) + CI check that the spec matches the Go handlers (drift detection).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- OpenAPI spec covers every endpoint the frontend calls (cross-check the ~53 wrapper functions)
- Generated types integrated; zero changes to domain-module export signatures (verify: the 36 jest.mock call sites untouched)
- npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, full Jest suite green
- Regeneration script + drift check in CI
- client.test.ts updated only if client internals genuinely changed

(EXECUTION STEPS added by the PM at dispatch.)
close_reason: OpenAPI 3.1 contract (backend/openapi/openapi.yaml) + bidirectional drift contract test + openapi-typescript types wired behind the unchanged api facade; CI drift check added. PM line-read + independent gates green (incl. full integration vs winzy-db). Worker: GreenMouse (grok-4.5-xhigh via ntm).

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
EXECUTION STEPS (PM SapphireLion, 2026-07-12). Ground truth: epic winzy.ai-rdc7 comments 1+2, PLUS the corrections in later epic comments: auth validation errors are 400 {"errors":{field:[msgs]}} (NOT 422), other modules use plain {"error"} bodies — always read the Go handlers as the source of truth for shapes, not the old C# comments.

PM DECISIONS (binding unless you message the PM with evidence for a better option BEFORE diverging):
- HAND-WRITTEN OpenAPI 3.1 spec at backend/openapi/openapi.yaml + Go contract tests for drift. Rationale: stdlib net/http has no annotation-native generator; the Go handlers are already the parity-verified truth; a spec + bidirectional route drift test drifts less for a solo repo than comment annotations.
- TS types via openapi-typescript (types-only, zero runtime), generated into frontend/src/api/generated/ and CHECKED IN (CI diffs against regeneration). Verify openapi-typescript's current CLI/usage via Context7 or official docs before use — do not guess flags.

STEPS
1. Enumerate the real API surface: read each backend/internal/*/routes.go + cmd/api/main.go (publicRoutes, /health). Cross-check against ALL 13 frontend/src/api/* modules (~53 wrapper functions) — every endpoint the frontend calls must be covered. Old internal-only endpoints don't exist in Go; don't invent them.
2. Write backend/openapi/openapi.yaml: paths, methods, path/query params, required headers (X-Timezone where the Go handler requires it), request/response schemas with EXACT JSON field names from the Go models, bearerAuth security scheme with public routes marked, and per-module error responses as ACTUALLY implemented in Go (409 {"error"}, auth 400 {"errors"}, plain {"error"} elsewhere).
3. Test BOTH directions: (a) every spec path+method pattern-matches a registered handler via the stdlib mux (mux.Handler(req) — never invoke handlers), (b) every registered route appears in the spec — use a recording registrar wrapper so registration cannot silently bypass the check. A hand-maintained duplicate route list is NOT acceptable drift detection.
4. Generate TS types: npm script "generate:api-types". Rewire frontend/src/api/* INTERNALS ONLY: alias/re-derive the handwritten request/response interfaces from generated types. HARD CONSTRAINTS: every exported name, signature, and return type of the domain modules stays identical; the 36 jest.mock domain-module sites and all call sites stay untouched; client.ts behavior byte-compatible (Bearer, credentials include, single-flight 401 refresh + one retry, retry 429/502/503/504 on safe methods only, 15s timeout, 204→undefined, ApiError mapping incl. 409 {"error"} and {"errors"} dict).
5. Replace every "Keep in sync with: services/.../*.cs" comment with a pointer to the Go source file or the spec.
6. CI: ci-frontend.yml gets a drift step (regenerate types + git diff --exit-code on the generated dir); the Go contract test rides ci-go.yml automatically. Minimal CI changes.
7. Gates: backend gofmt -l / go vet ./... / go build ./... (NEVER go build ./cmd/api — no binaries in the tree) / go test ./... (unit). This bead should not need -tags=integration; if you believe otherwise, message the PM first (ONE integration runner against winzy-db, ever). Frontend: npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test. If lint fails for PRE-EXISTING eslint-config reasons, report it verbatim — do not migrate eslint config in this bead.
8. Report on this thread: files changed, spec-coverage cross-check (frontend module → endpoints → spec), decision justifications, FULL gate output, deviations, open questions. Then release reservations. Never commit, never push, never close beads, no br write commands.

title: Same-origin web serving + E2E green against Go
description:
Make the Go binary serve the Expo web bundle same-origin and prove the whole product works end-to-end against the Go stack. Ground truth: epic winzy.ai-rdc7 comment 2 (WEB SERVING + E2E sections).

WHY SAME-ORIGIN MATTERS: the production frontend is built with EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL empty -> every API call is same-origin. If the Go server does not serve the SPA and the API on one origin, the deployed app simply does not work.

TASKS
1. Static serving in Go replicating the gateway behavior: serve the "npx expo export --platform web" output (frontend/dist) + frontend/assets; API routes take PRECEDENCE over static; SPA fallback to index.html for unknown non-API paths (e.g. /@username); framework-default caching semantics are fine (ETag/Last-Modified) - the C# sets no explicit cache headers.
2. Production Dockerfile.railway equivalent for backend/: multi-stage node build of the Expo bundle -> Go build -> minimal runtime image with the bundle embedded or copied.
3. Local full-stack mode: docker compose profile or documented procedure that runs Go + winzy-db with the Go server reachable on http://localhost:5050 (stop the old gateway first - E2E hardcodes :5050 in fixtures/auth.setup.ts, notifications.spec.ts, create-challenge.spec.ts, friends.spec.ts; do NOT edit the specs, meet them).
4. Run the FULL gate suite against the Go stack and paste results: npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, Jest, and all 11 Playwright E2E specs.
5. Known pre-existing flag to check while here: frontend/public/sw.js fetch-bypass prefix list is missing /activity and /witness - verify behavior against the Go stack and report (fix only if actually broken; it is a pre-existing condition).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Same-origin serving verified: production-built bundle served by Go, register/login/habit flows work in a real browser with no EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL set
- SPA fallback works for /@username deep links
- All 11 E2E specs green against Go on :5050 (paste run output)
- Jest + lint + tsc green
- Railway-ready Dockerfile builds and runs

(EXECUTION STEPS added by the PM at dispatch.)
close_reason: Same-origin serving (web.SplitHandler/SPAHandler, WEB_DIST-gated), Dockerfile.railway (distroless, repo-root context), compose profile go on :5050. E2E 45 passed/2 skipped/0 failed + same-origin proof 20 passed + /auth/export verified. PM line-read + independent gates green (unit+integration+frontend). Worker: GreenMouse.

The report you paste back includes the per-spec pass/fail table, not just an exit code. title: Thread the delete transaction through UserDeleted cascade handlers
description:
Found during the PM deep review (2026-07-09): auth's DeleteAccount deletes the user row inside a transaction and emits UserDeleted before commit, but the habits UserDeleted handler (rdc7.3.1) runs its DELETEs on its own pool connection OUTSIDE that transaction. Failure window: if the handler succeeds but the auth tx commit then fails, the user's habits are gone while the account survives. Today the window is small; after beads 4-7 there will be FIVE cascade handlers and the window grows with each.

WHY NOW: the mechanism must exist BEFORE winzy.ai-rdc7.4 dispatches, so social/challenges/notifications/activity write their handlers against it instead of retrofitting five modules later.

DESIGN (PM decision, refine details in implementation): a querier-through-context mechanism in internal/db - e.g. db.WithQuerier(ctx, tx) + db.QuerierFrom(ctx, fallbackPool). Emitters that hold a transaction put it in ctx before events.Emit; store functions in cascade paths resolve their querier via QuerierFrom(ctx, s.pool) instead of using s.pool directly. Since every module shares the one Postgres, a single transaction covering user delete + all cascades is now possible and is the whole point of the monolith move (the epic's TARGET ARCHITECTURE promises exactly this).

SCOPE:
- internal/db: the context mechanism + tests
- internal/auth DeleteAccount: put the tx in ctx before emitting UserDeleted
- internal/habits handleUserDeleted + deleteUserData: resolve querier from ctx
- Document the pattern in internal/events' doc comment (handlers that write MUST use QuerierFrom) - this is the contract beads 4-7 inherit
- Integration test: a UserDeleted handler failure rolls back BOTH the user row and the habits rows; a commit-path test proves habits rows are gone only when the user row is

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Account deletion + habits cascade are atomic (proven by the rollback test)
- Pattern documented where module beads will find it (events doc comment + a line in backend/README)
- No behavior change on any other endpoint; all existing tests green

(EXECUTION STEPS added by the PM at dispatch.)
close_reason: Cascade tx-threading implemented (worker-rdc7-13/Sonnet), PM-reviewed: db.WithQuerier/QuerierFrom mechanism, auth delete + habits cascade now atomic (rollback + commit + no-tx-fallback integration tests), contract documented for the four modules still to come. See PM REVIEW comment.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
DISPATCH: EXECUTION STEPS (PM, 2026-07-09 - follow in order)
1. CONTEXT: read CLAUDE.md ("PM / Worker Split" binds you); br show winzy.ai-rdc7.13; br show winzy.ai-rdc7 + comments (architecture); the PM REVIEW comments on rdc7.2 and rdc7.3.1; then read the code you change: internal/db, internal/events (doc + registry), internal/auth/service.go DeleteAccount (its doc comment describes the exact gap you are closing), internal/habits service.go handleUserDeleted + store.go deleteUserData.
2. MECHANISM in internal/db: a Querier interface (Exec/QueryRow/Query - structurally identical to the private querier interfaces auth and habits already declare; keep theirs, they are satisfied automatically) + WithQuerier(ctx, q) and QuerierFrom(ctx, fallback Querier) Querier. Unit tests: fallback when unset, override when set, nested override.
3. auth DeleteAccount: wrap ctx with WithQuerier(ctx, tx) before events.Emit, and REWRITE its doc comment - the honest-gap paragraph gets replaced by a description of the now-actual atomicity (delete + all registered cascades commit or roll back together).
4. habits handleUserDeleted: resolve the querier via db.QuerierFrom(ctx, s.pool) and pass it to deleteUserData.
5. CONTRACT DOCS: internal/events package doc gains the rule every later module inherits (beads 4-7 register four more UserDeleted handlers against this): cascade handlers that WRITE must resolve their querier via db.QuerierFrom(ctx, fallback) - never a raw pool - so they join the emitter's transaction when one exists. One line in backend/README's conventions too.
6. INTEGRATION TESTS (the point of the bead - use the dbtest recipe):
   a. ROLLBACK: register an intentionally-failing extra UserDeleted handler in the test registry (after the habits one), call DeleteAccount -> assert the user row AND the user's habits/completions ALL still exist (previously the habits rows would have been lost).
   b. COMMIT: without the failing handler, DeleteAccount -> user row, habits, completions all gone.
   c. No-tx path: emitting UserDeleted without a tx in ctx still works against the pool (fallback).
7. Full gates (paste): gofmt -l empty; go vet -tags=integration ./...; go build; go test -race ./...; TEST_DATABASE_URL=... go test -tags=integration -race -count=1 -p 1 ./... (all packages - auth AND habits are both touched).

title: Flaky auth integration test: ChangePassword intermittent 404 in full-suite runs
description: Reported by the flame-engine worker (2026-07-09): during a full -p 1 integration run, TestChangePassword_* failed once with 'PUT /auth/password status 404', then passed on a clean re-run and in isolation. Suspected test-isolation issue INSIDE the auth package (dbtest.Connect truncates every table per test - if any test in the package runs with t.Parallel, or the testserver shares state across subtests, a truncation can wipe another in-flight test's user mid-flow). Per the never-dismiss-flaky-tests rule: investigate and fix the isolation root cause; do NOT retry-loop or delete the test. Acceptance: root cause identified and fixed; 5 consecutive full -p 1 integration runs green.
close_reason: Root cause: cross-process truncate CASCADE during ChangePassword's Argon2 TOCTOU window. Fix: Postgres session advisory lock in dbtest.Connect (dedicated conn, unlock in Cleanup, auto-release on death) — serializes integration suites across packages AND agents. Verified: ChangePassword -count=20 green (380s) + 3x consecutive full -race suites green (269/274/311s). Workers: GreenMouse (hypothesis), RusticRaven (fix+verification).

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
EXECUTION STEPS (PM SapphireLion, 2026-07-12). ANALYSIS-FIRST, DB-GATED.

1. Read this bead fully + backend/internal/auth's ChangePassword integration test and handler/service/store paths it exercises. The symptom: intermittent 404 in FULL-suite runs only (-tags=integration -race -p 1 ./...), never reported in isolated package runs — think cross-test state, truncation timing, token/session reuse across tests, or ordering assumptions.
2. Produce a written root-cause hypothesis WITH evidence (line references, the exact interleaving) on this thread BEFORE any fix. If you need repro runs: message the PM for a DB GO first (another worker holds a window on the winzy-db instance; your repro also must not run while its parity run is active — instance-level noise cuts both ways).
3. Fix = minimal, in test code if the test is at fault, in product code ONLY if you prove a real race (that would be a product bug — flag it loudly).
4. Verification after GO: the affected test in a loop (e.g. -count=20) AND 3 consecutive full integration suite runs, all green. Rule from memory: never dismiss as "pre-existing flake", never delete/weaken the test.
5. Gates: standard backend set. Report: root cause, evidence, fix diff summary, full verification output. Never commit/push/close beads/br writes.

comments.text: PM APPROVAL (SapphireLion, 2026-07-12): GreenMouse's hypothesis CONFIRMED as root cause — truncate CASCADE from a concurrent process during ChangePassword's Argon2 TOCTOU window (dbtest doc comment lines 24-28 already documented this class). Approved fix shape: Postgres session advisory lock acquired in dbtest.Connect on a dedicated connection, released in Cleanup — serializes integration suites across packages AND processes/agents; auto-releases on connection death. Do NOT weaken tests, no retry loops. Implementation handed to a fresh worker (GreenMouse retired at 20% context). NOTE for the future: once this lands, the manual PM GO protocol for integration runs may be relaxed (lock does the serialization) — keep the GO protocol until proven.

Concretely: a date-line owner's day-ahead completion shows as a completed date in the UTC share view whose flame counts it as 0. Cosmetic inconsistency, intended-contract adjacent. title: Auth + infrastructure hardening: 2026-07-12 whole-backend adversarial sweep findings
description:
Findings from the 2026-07-12 whole-backend adversarial sweep (gpt-5.6-sol, high reasoning, full backend/ tree at 70d1992). PM verified the four majors against source before filing; minors get verified during fix review. Concentrated in internal/auth + infrastructure — the code written before the strict per-bead review standard matured. Fix all below; the C# auth-service remains the parity spec where applicable.

MAJORS (verified):
1. JWT validation too permissive — internal/auth/token.go:120: parser accepts ANY *jwt.SigningMethodHMAC (HS256/384/512) instead of exactly HS256, and does not require exp (jwt/v5 needs explicit jwt.WithExpirationRequired()); sub is not validated as a non-empty UUID, so a key-holder-minted malformed-sub token passes middleware and 500s in handlers. C#: TokenValidationParameters RequireExpirationTime=true + HS256. Fix: jwt.WithValidMethods(["HS256"]), WithExpirationRequired, reject empty/non-UUID sub with the same 401 path. Port the C# tests for these.
2. Password length counted in UTF-8 bytes + missing whitespace rule — internal/auth/validation.go:47: len(password) vs C#'s .Length (UTF-16 code units), and the C# rejects whitespace-only passwords (verify the exact C# check in AuthEndpoints/validators before implementing — match it verbatim incl. error strings). Same bug class fixed twice already (promise notes rdc7.3.3, witness labels rdc7.4) — use habits.UTF16Len.
3. No request-body size limit — internal/auth/handlers.go decodeJSON (and every other module's): no MaxBytesReader anywhere; old Kestrel had a ~28.6MB default cap, Go is unbounded -> multi-GB JSON body forces unbounded allocation pre-validation. Fix globally: http.MaxBytesHandler (or MaxBytesReader in the shared middleware) at a sane limit (1MB is generous for every endpoint in this API), 413 on breach; decide + document the error shape (C# returns 413 RequestEntityTooLarge from Kestrel with empty body).
4. No body/write timeouts — internal/httpserver/server.go: only ReadHeaderTimeout(5s); slow-drip bodies hold connections+goroutines through shutdown. Fix: ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout on the http.Server (pick values generous for the SVG/export endpoints, e.g. read 30s / write 60s / idle 120s) — flame.svg and /auth/export are the slowest legitimate paths.
5. Rate limiter keyed on RemoteAddr behind a reverse proxy — internal/ratelimit/limiter.go:93 ClientIP deliberately ignores X-Forwarded-For (fine locally, WRONG on Railway where every request arrives from the proxy IP): all users share one bucket -> 10 attacker login attempts 429 every real user for the window; also per-IP limiting is meaningless. Fix config-gated: TRUSTED_PROXY env (default false), when true take the FIRST untrusted hop from X-Forwarded-For (or Railway's specific header contract — VERIFY what Railway actually sets before implementing); keep RemoteAddr otherwise. Tests for both modes incl. spoofed-header-when-untrusted.

MINORS:
6. Auth decodeJSON accepts literal null bodies and trailing garbage (internal/auth/handlers.go:88; logout discards decode errors at ~166) — bring auth up to the three-way decode convention habits/social now share (decodeOK/decodeNull/decodeMalformed + per-endpoint C# null-check mapping; PUT /auth/profile null body must 400 "Request body is required" if that's what the C# does — verify each endpoint).
7. Sec-Fetch-Site web detection tests header VALUE non-empty; C# tests header PRESENCE (ContainsKey) — internal/auth/handlers.go:32. An empty-valued header currently gets the refresh token in the JSON body instead of cookie-only. Match C#.
8. Blank displayName normalized to NULL; C# trims to "" — internal/auth/service.go:114. Match C# (response shows "" not null).
9. User search validates length AFTER trimming; C# checks BEFORE — internal/auth/service.go:367: query " a " returns results in C#, empty in Go. Match C#.
10. Recovery middleware wraps outside RequestLogging, so a panic mid-request skips the normal request log line (and a 200-then-panic appends an error body) — internal/httpserver/middleware.go:45. At minimum ensure a panicking request still emits ONE complete request log with status+duration; document the 200-then-panic body caveat (unfixable once bytes are flushed).
11. Config duration fields can overflow time.Duration — internal/config/config.go:102: cap JWT_ACCESS_TOKEN_MINUTES / JWT_REFRESH_TOKEN_DAYS (and any other multiplied durations) at sane maxima with fail-fast errors.

FILED AS KNOWN, NOT FIXED HERE: a deleted account's access token stays valid up to 15 min (stateless JWT) — the OLD stack had the identical property (gateway validated statelessly, services trusted X-User-Id), so this is parity-equal, not a regression; revisit post-cutover only if it ever matters at real user scale.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every major fixed with tests (happy/edge/error per CLAUDE.md); JWT tests prove HS512 + no-exp + garbage-sub tokens are rejected with the C# 401 shape.
- Auth module body handling matches the habits/social three-way decode convention with per-endpoint C#-verified null behavior.
- Rate limiting verified in both proxy modes; body-limit + timeouts covered by tests where testable (httptest for 413; timeouts by construction/inspection).
- Full gates green (gofmt, vet both variants, build, unit -count=1, integration -race -count=1 -p 1 — ONE runner at a time per backend/README).
- No behavior change for any existing passing test except where a fix deliberately corrects it (list each).

(EXECUTION STEPS added by the PM at dispatch.)
close_reason: All 11 sweep findings implemented + 3 fix-round issues (BodyLimit ordering, X-Real-IP last-value, chunked-empty-body decode); PM line-read complete, worker+PM integration runs green under -race, adversarial delta pass clean. TRUSTED_PROXY=true noted for Railway cutover.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
EXECUTION STEPS (PM dispatch, 2026-07-12 — FIRST AGENT MAIL PILOT, Codex/gpt-5.6-sol worker). The bead description above IS the finding list — 5 majors + 6 minors with file:line and fix guidance; work top to bottom, majors first.

ORDER: majors 1-5 (JWT strictness, UTF-16+whitespace password validation, MaxBytes body limit, server timeouts, proxy-aware rate limiting), then minors 6-11. Each fix: verify the exact C# behavior in services/auth-service/src BEFORE implementing (error strings verbatim; auth uses the {"errors":{field:[msgs]}} 400 shape, unlike habits/social). Reuse existing machinery: habits.UTF16Len for finding 2, the habits/social three-way decodeJSON pattern for finding 6 (auth gets its own copy matching its error shape), isPublicRoute-style config plumbing for finding 5's TRUSTED_PROXY.

CONVENTIONS (binding): CLAUDE.md Testing section (happy/edge/error per area); integration tests via internal/dbtest recipe, build tag integration; match surrounding comment density; no new file variants; verify library APIs (jwt/v5: WithValidMethods, WithExpirationRequired) against the vendored source in go.mod's module cache, not memory.

(1) After registering, send VioletJaguar a hello on thread winzy.ai-n5fv. Unit tests (no tag) need no permission. (5) Final report as a reply on the thread: files per finding, decisions, C# deviations, full gate output, open questions. (6) release_file_reservations after the report.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
PM review record (SapphireLion, 2026-07-12) — APPROVED.

Scope: 11 findings from the whole-backend adversarial sweep (gpt-5.6-sol over 70d1992), all implemented. Worker: Codex gpt-5.6-sol "NobleGoose" (round 1), ported mid-round to a Claude session "MagentaCastle" via casr after an OpenAI quota outage — same Agent Mail thread, zero context loss.

Round 1 (NobleGoose): all 11 findings implemented with C# receipts spot-verified by the PM (ValidationFilter.cs:25-40 password rules, AuthEndpoints.cs:67 displayName trim, :131-190 nullable RefreshRequest?, :322-344 pre-trim search length, :507-524 Sec-Fetch-Site ContainsKey; TokenService.cs HS256/exp/UUID-sub issuance). Worker self-caught a logout regression on its first integration run (C# nullable body → decodeOptionalJSON). PM line-read of the full diff: complete; PM independent gates: green.

PM adversarial pass (gpt-5.6-sol over the round-1 diff) found 3 ordering/correctness issues → fix round:
- FIX A: BodyLimit ran before rate limiting and outside CORS, and eagerly buffered bodyless methods. Now wired in cmd/api/main.go between the limiter and auth (RequestID → RequestLogging → Recovery → CORS → RateLimit → BodyLimit → Auth → mux); POST/PUT/PATCH eager-buffer only, everything else lazy MaxBytesReader. Mirrored into all 3 integration harnesses.
- FIX B: X-Real-IP trusted the FIRST header value (Header.Get) — append-style proxies would let an attacker rotate buckets. Now takes the LAST via Header.Values + net.ParseIP validation.
- FIX C: decodeOptionalJSON consulted r.ContentLength, stale/-1 for chunked bodies after BodyLimit buffering. Now decides emptiness from actual body bytes; chunked-empty-body integration tests added (refresh 401, logout 204).

Notably, the resumed worker independently re-derived all 3 issues from its own adversarial pass before reading the fix spec.

Delta verified line-by-line in the diff; new tests reviewed (7 middleware incl. panic-log + committed-status edge cases, 6 limiter incl. both proxy modes + spoofing); worker integration run green under -race; PM exclusive final gates green (gofmt, vet both variants, build, unit -count=1, integration -race -count=1 -p 1). DB mutex protocol held throughout — zero contaminated runs.

Deployment note: set TRUSTED_PROXY=true in Railway at cutover (rdc7.10); code defaults false safely.
Known-not-fixed (parity-equal, documented in bead): deleted account's access token stays valid ≤15 min — identical property in the old stack.

title: Habits: accept C# JsonStringEnumConverter input forms (customDays day-names, completionKind strings)
description:
Parity findings F4+F5 from the rdc7.12 phase-2 Go-diff (thread winzy.ai-rdc7.12, message 94): the C# stack's JsonStringEnumConverter accepted BOTH integers and string enum names on input; the Go port only accepts integers, returning 400 'Invalid JSON in request body'.

F4: POST /habits with customDays:["monday","wednesday","friday"] -> old stack 201 (ints [1,3,5] persisted), Go 400. Go CustomDays is []int.
F5: PUT /habits/{id}/completions/{date} with {"completionKind":"minimum"|"full"} -> old stack 200, Go 400. (POST /complete integer form works; frontend sends integers everywhere, E2E green - zero product impact today, but the API surface must match the C# spec.)

FIX: custom UnmarshalJSON on the request-side types so both forms decode: customDays elements accept int 0-6 OR case-insensitive English day names (C# DayOfWeek: sunday=0..saturday=6 - VERIFY the C# numbering against DayOfWeek docs and the golden [1,3,5]=mon/wed/fri); completionKind accepts int (1=full,2=minimum) OR string name (case per C# converter, camelCase policy). Invalid values keep the existing error shapes. Response-side JSON must NOT change (OpenAPI spec + goldens pin it). Update backend/openapi/openapi.yaml request schemas (oneOf int|string) - the contract test and TS generation must stay green (regenerate types if schema changed).

ALSO (rdc7.9 relevance, document in your report): state clearly which representation C# EF persisted for Habit.CustomDays jsonb (ints or day-name strings) by reading the C# serializer config (JsonDefaults/EF value converter) - the migration transform depends on it.

ACCEPTANCE: table-driven unit tests covering both input forms + invalid values for both endpoints; parity scenarios habits-crud-archive, completions-backfill-and-kinds, challenges-all-milestone-types, notifications-lifecycle expected to pass on rerun; full standard gates.
close_reason: Both C# JsonStringEnumConverter input forms accepted (customDays day-names, completionKind strings); openapi request schemas oneOf; PM line-read + unit/tsc/jest gates green; parity rerun will confirm. Worker: VioletJay (one stale-regen catch fixed by PM).

title: EPIC: backend test gate 20-35x faster, zero coverage loss
description:
Make the full backend gate (unit + integration) dramatically faster with ZERO coverage loss. Honest target: 20-35x on the full gate (5.5 min -> ~10-20s), with listed future levers beyond that. This epic is self-contained: baseline, physics, plan, and rules are here; children carry implementation detail.

== CHILDREN (real IDs — use these, no other naming) ==
PHASE A (independent of each other, no deps):
  winzy.ai-o5cd — Argon2 test-tuned params + production pin test (P1, biggest lever)
  winzy.ai-r1pf — test-Postgres write-speed flags (fsync off etc.)
  winzy.ai-b8z5 — dbtest fast-path (migrate-once, cached table list)
PHASE B: winzy.ai-edxi — per-package test databases, drop -p 1 (P1; blocked by b8z5)
PHASE C: winzy.ai-utzz — in-package t.Parallel design + habits pilot (blocked by edxi)
         winzy.ai-zfa3 — rollout to all packages (blocked by utzz)
CLOSE-OUT: winzy.ai-56ki — CI timing report + tripwire, docs, skill updates (blocked by edxi; re-verify after zfa3)

== MEASURED BASELINE (2026-07-12, M-series dev machine, 3 fresh runs during rdc7.14 verification) ==
- Full integration (-tags=integration -race -p 1 ./...): 269s / 274s / 311s
- Per-package integration: auth 131s, challenges 37s, activity 31s, notifications 16.5s, social 15.7s, habits 14s, health/httpserver/config/db ~1.4s each
- Unit (go test ./..., uncached): ~25s total (auth ~2.8s dominated by Argon2 unit tests)
- Combined full gate: ~5-5.5 min wall-clock

== WHERE THE TIME ACTUALLY GOES (verified in code, not guessed) ==
1. ARGON2 AT PRODUCTION STRENGTH: backend/internal/auth/password.go hardcodes parallelism=1, memoryKiB=65536, iterations=3 (~100ms+ per hash). EVERY module's testserver registers users through the real API -> every package pays it. Auth's 131s is mostly hashing.
2. SERIAL PACKAGES: -p 1 (ci-go.yml:73, backend/README, test-backend.sh) because all packages share ONE database (winzy on winzy-db:5439) and dbtest.Truncate wipes ALL tables per test. Wall-clock = SUM instead of MAX. rdc7.14's advisory lock makes cross-process runs safe but still serializes.
3. PER-TEST OVERHEAD: dbtest.Connect (dbtest.go:73-117) runs db.Migrate() + pg_tables query + TRUNCATE ... RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE per test.
4. DURABLE WRITES FOR THROWAWAY DATA: stock postgres pays WAL-flush durability for data nobody needs after the test.
5. NO IN-PACKAGE PARALLELISM: zero t.Parallel() anywhere (single shared DB makes it unsafe today).

== EXPECTED OUTCOMES (honest math) ==
- After Phase A: full integration ~300s -> ~60-80s (4-5x), still serial.
- After Phase B: wall-clock = slowest package -> combined gate ~25-35s (10-15x).
- After Phase C: slowest package parallelized -> full gate ~10-20s (20-35x vs baseline). Single-package dev loop (habits) 14s -> ~2-4s.
- FUTURE LEVERS beyond this epic (explicitly OUT of scope, listed so nobody oversells): CI test sharding across runners; local iteration without -race while CI keeps it (policy change, needs owner sign-off); Go test result caching already gives ~0s no-change reruns.

== NON-NEGOTIABLE RULES (bind every child) ==
- -race stays in CI and pre-push FOREVER (it is not a speed lever).
- No test deleted, skipped, or weakened; no assertion loosened. Coverage-neutrality is the acceptance bar of every child.
- Exactly ONE test pins production Argon2 params as a contract (winzy.ai-o5cd).
- After each phase: 3 consecutive full-suite green runs (the rdc7.14 standard) + before/after timing table in the bead's close comment.
- The winzy_parity / winzy_rehearsal / winzy-mig-db databases and instances are untouched by all of this.

== VERIFIED FACTS CHILDREN RELY ON ==
- pg_advisory_lock is scoped PER DATABASE in Postgres -> per-package databases automatically give per-package locks with the unchanged key 0x57696e7a79444254; no re-key needed (winzy.ai-edxi).
- fsync, synchronous_commit, full_page_writes are all sighup-context GUCs -> settable at runtime via ALTER SYSTEM + pg_reload_conf(), no restart (winzy.ai-r1pf's CI mechanism).
- golang-migrate is invoked via db.Migrate(url) (backend/internal/db); schema_migrations bookkeeping exists per database.
- Store code uses unqualified table names + pgxpool -> search_path schema isolation is viable (winzy.ai-utzz option b).
- go test runs each package's test binary with CWD = the package source dir -> os.Getwd() basename identifies the package (winzy.ai-edxi).
- CI Postgres is a service container (ci-go.yml); compose winzy-db is postgres:17-alpine at 5439.
close_reason: Epic complete: backend test gate ~300s (pre-epic, -p 1 serial) → ~9-11s full -race suite via per-package DBs (Phase A+B) + per-test template clones with in-package t.Parallel (Phase C). Zero tests deleted or weakened at any phase.

Per-package after: auth 13.1s, habits 7.0s, social 6.4s, activity 3.0s, challenges 2.9s, notifications 2.5s. title: Argon2 test-tuned hashing params + production-params pin test
description:
PHASE A of winzy.ai-s8ly (read the epic's baseline + rules first). Biggest single lever: ~100ms+ per hash at parallelism=1/memoryKiB=65536/iterations=3 (backend/internal/auth/password.go:24-26), paid by EVERY package because every module testserver registers users through the real API.

IMPLEMENTATION
1. password.go: keep the production values as named CONSTANTS (prodParallelism=1, prodMemoryKiB=65536, prodIterations=3) and introduce an unexported package var hashingParams initialized from those constants. Hash/verify read the var.
2. Add SetHashingParamsForTests(parallelism uint8, memoryKiB, iterations uint32) in a NEW file password_testhooks.go — plain exported func; doc comment states it exists for test processes only and MUST be called exactly once, from TestMain, BEFORE any test runs (it mutates a package var: calling it per-test is a data race once t.Parallel arrives in winzy.ai-utzz/zfa3, and -race will rightly fail it). No build tags — other packages' _test files must be able to call it.
3. Weak test params: 1 / 1024 KiB / 1 iteration. Call sites: TestMain (or the shared setup entry) of every package with a testserver_integration_test.go (auth, habits, social, challenges, notifications, activity — grep newTestServer) + auth's own TestMain for its unit tests.
4. PIN TEST (the coverage guarantee, ORDER-INDEPENDENT BY DESIGN): TestProductionHashingParamsPinned in the auth package asserts the CONSTANTS — not the runtime var — are exactly 1/65536/3, then performs one real hash+verify round-trip constructed explicitly from those constants (bypassing the var), asserting the stored format base64(salt16):base64(hash32). This works no matter what TestMain set the var to. A silent production-param regression must fail THIS test.
5. Prove the pin once: temporarily edit a constant locally, watch the pin test fail, revert; state this in the report.
6. Do NOT touch: stored-format contract, VerifyPassword logic, tools/migrate/internal/authcheck (it pins PROD params independently — leave it).

ACCEPTANCE
- Full integration green; auth integration drops ~131s -> <25s, challenges -> <15s (exact before/after per package in the report).
- Pin test present, constants-based, demonstrated to fail on param edit.
- SetHashingParamsForTests called only from TestMain sites (grep-verifiable), never per-test.
- No test deleted/weakened; -race unchanged; 3x consecutive full-suite green.
close_reason: Constants-pinned prod params (1/65536/3) + injectable hashingParams var; SetHashingParamsForTests called once per TestMain (grep-verified); pin test asserts constants + constants-based round-trip, failure-demonstrated; .NET hash fixtures moved to prod-constants tests. RESULTS: full gate 269-311s -> ~47s (5-6x), auth 131->13.1s, challenges 37->2.9s; 3x green. Worker: SageFrog.

title: Test Postgres write-speed flags: fsync=off, synchronous_commit=off, full_page_writes=off
description:
PHASE A of winzy.ai-s8ly (read the epic first). The winzy-db TEST instance pays WAL-flush durability for throwaway data. Standard test-instance tuning; zero test-semantics impact, zero coverage impact — purely write latency.

KEY FACT (verified, in the epic): fsync, synchronous_commit, and full_page_writes are all sighup-context GUCs — changeable at RUNTIME via ALTER SYSTEM SET ... + SELECT pg_reload_conf(), no server restart. This is what makes the CI mechanism simple.

IMPLEMENTATION
1. docker-compose.yml winzy-db service: command: postgres -c fsync=off -c synchronous_commit=off -c full_page_writes=off (comment: TEST instance only — trades crash-durability for speed; NEVER copy to production/Railway/winzy-mig-db).
2. ci-go.yml: GitHub Actions service containers do NOT support command overrides — do NOT fight that. Instead add one step right after the service is healthy:
   psql "$TEST_DATABASE_URL" -c "ALTER SYSTEM SET fsync=off" -c "ALTER SYSTEM SET synchronous_commit=off" -c "ALTER SYSTEM SET full_page_writes=off" -c "SELECT pg_reload_conf()"
   (single psql invocation is fine; verify with SHOW fsync afterwards and echo it into the log).
3. OPTIONAL EXTRA (measure, keep if it helps): service container options: --tmpfs /var/lib/postgresql/data — RAM-backed data dir in CI. Note: mount path differs on postgres:18 images (/var/lib/postgresql); ours is 17 — verify against the image actually used in CI.
4. NOT for: winzy-mig-db (rehearsal data has value), Railway, backend/Dockerfile*.
5. Measure: 3 full integration runs before/after; STATE whether winzy.ai-o5cd was already merged (avoid conflated numbers — either order is fine, just say which).

ACCEPTANCE: flags verified active locally + CI (SHOW fsync output in a log once); before/after timing table; 3x full-suite green; compose comment warns against prod use.
close_reason: Flags active on winzy-db (compose command + never-prod comment) and CI (tmpfs data dir + ALTER SYSTEM/pg_reload_conf step with SHOW verification — GHA service containers cannot override command). HONEST RESULT: local wall-clock delta = noise (~48s both sides, 3x green each) — after Argon2 tuning + migrate-once the local suite is no longer write-latency-bound. Kept because: zero risk, CI runners (slower IO) + tmpfs are the expected beneficiaries, and standard practice. Worker: RainyBrook.

title: dbtest fast-path: migrate-once per process+database, cached table list
description:
PHASE A of winzy.ai-s8ly (read the epic first). dbtest.Connect (backend/internal/dbtest/dbtest.go:73-117) currently pays per TEST: db.Migrate(url) round-trips + a pg_tables catalog query + TRUNCATE ... RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE over all tables. With hundreds of tests that fixed overhead adds up and it all serializes under the advisory lock.

IMPLEMENTATION
1. Migrate-once: package-level sync.Map keyed by database URL -> sync.Once; first Connect in the process runs db.Migrate, the rest skip. (Safe: schema never changes mid-run; golang-migrate itself is also idempotent — this only removes its network round-trips.)
2. Cache the truncate table list per URL after first computation (tables only change when migrations change -> same process lifetime is safe). Invalidate nothing; a fresh process recomputes.
3. Keep TRUNCATE ... RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE semantics EXACTLY (tests rely on identity restart + clean slate). Do NOT switch to DELETE without measuring both under the s8ly.2 flags (TRUNCATE is typically faster for full-table clears; note the measurement either way).
4. The advisory lock flow (rdc7.14) stays byte-identical: lock -> truncate -> test -> unlock.
5. Measure per-Connect overhead before/after (a 100-iteration Connect benchmark or the habits package wall-clock delta).

DEPENDS ON: nothing (independent of s8ly.1/.2, coordinate merge order in reports).
BLOCKS: per-package databases (the create+migrate-on-demand path reuses the migrate-once machinery).

ACCEPTANCE: full integration green 3x consecutive; measured before/after per-Connect numbers in the close comment; zero semantic change to test isolation (truncate still per test, lock unchanged).
close_reason: migrate-once per process+URL + cached table list; truncate semantics and advisory-lock sequence byte-identical. Per-Connect overhead 48.5ms -> 31.1ms (n=100 microbench); 3x full integration green (47-53s combined-tree). Worker: RusticRaven.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: FRESH-EYES NOTE (PM, 2026-07-12): the bead named its dependent by title only — for precision: this bead BLOCKS winzy.ai-edxi (per-package databases), which reuses the migrate-once machinery. Dependency edge already exists in the graph.

title: Per-package test databases: parallel packages, drop -p 1
description:
PHASE B of winzy.ai-s8ly (read the epic first). The single shared winzy database forces -p 1 (wall-clock = SUM of packages). Give every package its own database and packages run in parallel: wall-clock = MAX (slowest package, ~20s after Phase A).

VERIFIED FACTS (from the epic): (1) pg_advisory_lock is scoped per database -> per-package databases automatically give per-package locks with the unchanged key 0x57696e7a79444254; cross-process safety WITHIN a package is preserved, unrelated packages stop queuing. No re-key needed. (2) go test runs each package's test binary with CWD = the package source directory -> os.Getwd() basename identifies the package robustly (survives -trimpath; runtime.Caller does NOT need to be involved).

IMPLEMENTATION
1. dbtest.Connect derives the database name: winzy_test_<sanitized basename of os.Getwd()> (e.g. winzy_test_auth), suffixed with a short FNV hash of the FULL package path to guard against basename collisions (cmd/api -> winzy_test_api_<hash>). Postgres identifier limit is 63 bytes — sanitize + truncate before the hash suffix.
2. Auto-create: parse TEST_DATABASE_URL, swap dbname to postgres (maintenance DB), attempt CREATE DATABASE; it cannot run in a transaction and has no IF NOT EXISTS — catch duplicate_database (SQLSTATE 42P04) and continue. Guard cross-process create races with an advisory lock ON THE MAINTENANCE CONNECTION keyed by a hash of the target db name.
3. Reuse winzy.ai-b8z5's migrate-once machinery per new database.
4. TEST_DATABASE_URL semantics unchanged for callers (still points at the winzy database; dbtest rewrites the dbname internally). backend/README Test section: -p 1 no longer required; document the winzy_test_* family and that dropping any of them is always safe.
5. Remove -p 1 from ALL of: ci-go.yml:73, backend/README, test-backend.sh (repo root), any pre-push hook config (grep the whole repo for 'p 1' near go test). Update .claude/skills/worker-dispatch/SKILL.md: integration runs no longer collide across packages/agents -> the PM GO protocol for INTEGRATION TESTS is retired; GO slots REMAIN for E2E (port 5050 + shared winzy DB via compose), parity (:5051/winzy_parity), and migration rehearsals (winzy-mig-db). Date the delta.
6. The shared winzy database stays (E2E compose stack uses it); parity/rehearsal instances untouched.

DEPENDS ON: winzy.ai-b8z5 (migrate-once). rdc7.14's lock is already on main.
BLOCKS: winzy.ai-utzz (in-package parallelism pilot).

ACCEPTANCE: go test -tags=integration -race ./... (NO -p 1) green 3x consecutive; wall-clock <=90s pre-Phase-C (report exact); winzy_test_* DBs auto-created from scratch on a wiped instance (prove once by dropping them all first); README/CI/test-backend.sh/skill all updated; no test deleted/weakened.
close_reason: Per-package winzy_test_* databases (Getwd basename + fnv32 suffix, 63-byte safe); auto-create via maintenance conn + per-dbname advisory lock + 42P04-tolerant CREATE; migrate-once reused; -p 1 dropped from CI/README/test-backend.sh; worker-dispatch skill: integration GO mutex retired, E2E/parity/mig lanes kept. VERIFIED: 7 DBs auto-created from wiped instance; 3x parallel green at 13s wall vs 50s -p1 control (~3.8x); full-gate now ~22x vs epic baseline. Worker: RainyBrook.

title: In-package t.Parallel: isolation design + habits pilot
description:
PHASE C of winzy.ai-s8ly (read the epic first). After per-package databases (winzy.ai-edxi), tests within a package still run serially because dbtest truncates the whole package database per test. This bead DESIGNS per-test isolation, proves it on ONE package (habits), and documents the pattern for the rollout (winzy.ai-zfa3). Do not convert other packages here.

CANDIDATE MECHANISMS (prototype a vs b, measure, pick ONE, justify):
(a) PER-TEST DATABASE via TEMPLATE CLONE: pre-migrate winzy_test_habits_tmpl once, then CREATE DATABASE winzy_test_habits_<suffix> TEMPLATE winzy_test_habits_tmpl per test (~10-50ms, the standard Postgres trick; template must have no active connections during clone — serialize clones with a short advisory lock on the maintenance conn if needed). With fsync=off (winzy.ai-r1pf) creation is cheap.
(b) PER-TEST SCHEMA: one database, each test gets schema t_<suffix> with search_path set in the pool config. Store code uses unqualified table names (verified in the epic) so this is sound IF golang-migrate applies migrations into the target schema — verify it honors search_path on the migration connection for unqualified CREATE TABLE; if it fights back, prefer (a).
REJECTED already (do not revisit): tx-rollback wrapping — product code manages its own transactions (in-tx event handlers, cascade tx), cannot nest.

SUFFIX RULE (cross-process correctness): suffixes MUST be process-unique — os.Getpid() + a per-process atomic counter (e.g. _12345_7). A plain counter collides the moment two agents run the same package simultaneously, which is exactly the scenario rdc7.14 taught us to respect.

CLEANUP: drop the per-test DB/schema in t.Cleanup. Crashed processes leave orphans — that is ACCEPTED; document that winzy_test_* orphans are always safe to drop manually, and add a tiny mop-up subcommand or documented one-liner (drop winzy_test_%_% databases with no active connections) — do NOT auto-drop by age (another live process's DBs must never be candidates; no-active-connections + name pattern is the safe filter, run manually).

PILOT SCOPE
1. Implement the chosen mechanism behind dbtest.ConnectParallel(t) (Connect stays as-is for unconverted packages during the transition).
2. Convert backend/internal/habits: t.Parallel() on every integration test + ConnectParallel. Any test that fails under parallelism is a FINDING (cross-test dependence = latent bug) — fix its isolation properly and report each one.
3. ConnectParallel needs NO per-test advisory lock under full isolation — document that Connect (shared-DB path) keeps its lock.
4. Measure: habits integration wall-clock before/after (expect ~4s -> ~1-2s post-Phase-A numbers; report against whatever the then-current baseline is), plus per-test overhead of the mechanism.

DEPENDS ON: winzy.ai-edxi. BLOCKS: winzy.ai-zfa3.
ACCEPTANCE: habits integration green with -race and t.Parallel 10x consecutive (parallel flake bar is higher); measured numbers + a written PARALLEL-TESTS section in backend/README that zfa3 can follow mechanically; no test deleted/weakened; other packages untouched.
close_reason: ConnectParallel (template-clone isolation) shipped + habits pilot converted: 124 tests parallel, 10/10 race-green, habits wall ~2.3x faster; rollout checklist ready for zfa3

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
EXECUTION STEPS (PM dispatch elaboration, 2026-07-16 — read the full bead description first; it defines the candidate mechanisms, suffix rule, cleanup policy, and acceptance bar):
1. Read backend/internal/dbtest (all files) + backend/README.md PARALLEL/test sections + the winzy.ai-s8ly epic notes in the bead. Understand Connect's current contract (package DB + advisory lock + truncate).
2. PROTOTYPE both mechanisms as tiny throwaway benchmarks (scratch dir, not committed): (a) CREATE DATABASE ... TEMPLATE winzy_test_habits_tmpl per test; (b) per-test schema with search_path — including the golang-migrate-into-schema verification the bead demands. Measure per-test setup cost of each (report numbers).
3. Pick ONE mechanism, justify in writing (the losing prototype's numbers go in the report too).
4. Implement dbtest.ConnectParallel(t) behind the chosen mechanism. Suffix = os.Getpid() + per-process atomic counter. Drop DB/schema in t.Cleanup. Connect stays untouched.
5. Add the documented orphan mop-up one-liner (no auto-drop by age; no-active-connections + winzy_test_%_% name filter) to backend/README.
6. Convert backend/internal/habits integration tests: t.Parallel() on every integration test + ConnectParallel. Every test that fails under parallelism is a FINDING — fix its isolation properly and list each one in the report (do not weaken/delete tests).
7. Write the PARALLEL-TESTS section in backend/README.md that zfa3 can follow mechanically (checklist form).
8. Measure: habits integration wall-clock before/after (-count=1), per-test mechanism overhead.
9. Gates: gofmt -l . empty; go vet -tags=integration ./...; go build ./...; TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgres://winzy:winzy@localhost:5439/winzy?sslmode=disable go test -tags=integration -race -count=1 ./internal/habits/ TEN TIMES consecutively green (parallel flake bar); full go test -tags=integration ./... once (other packages must stay green via untouched Connect).
10. STOP. Report on this thread: mechanism chosen + why + measured numbers, isolation findings fixed, files changed, full gate output. Never commit/push/close; no br writes; integration tests need no GO slot; message the PM for a GO before anything touching port 5050/E2E.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: DONE (2026-07-16, gpt-5.6-sol worker TurquoiseLotus via ntm+Agent Mail; PM line-read of dbtest + gates re-run independently: full suite -race -count=1 green, 3 extra consecutive habits runs 3.0-3.3s vs 6.8s baseline). MECHANISM: template-database clone (13.3ms setup / 2.8ms cleanup) beat per-test schema+migrate (39.5ms / 12.0ms) 3x; schema variant verified workable (golang-migrate honors search_path) but rejected on speed. dbtest.ConnectParallel: pre-migrated per-package template (ensured once per process, cross-process maintenance advisory lock), clone name = pkg + pid + atomic counter (identifier truncation preserves path-hash tail, unit-tested), drop in t.Cleanup, no test-lifetime lock/truncate; Connect untouched. 124 habits tests converted, ZERO isolation findings, 10/10 consecutive -race runs green. README gained the zfa3 checklist + orphan mop-up one-liner (no-active-connections + name filter, never age-based).

title: t.Parallel rollout to all remaining integration packages
description:
PHASE C rollout of winzy.ai-s8ly. Apply the pilot's documented pattern (the PARALLEL-TESTS section of backend/README, written by winzy.ai-utzz) to every remaining integration package: auth, social, challenges, notifications, activity, export, events, health, httpserver, db, and cmd/api if any of its tests touch the DB.

RULES
1. Mechanical conversion per package: t.Parallel() on every integration test + dbtest.ConnectParallel. Report per-package so review is per-package.
2. Any test that fails under parallelism is a FINDING (cross-test dependence = latent bug): fix the test's isolation properly — never serialize it back quietly, never weaken assertions. If a genuine shared global resource emerges (none known today), keep that ONE test on the serial Connect path with written justification in the report.
3. auth deserves care: token rotation and rate-limit tests may assume wall-clock ordering; fix with per-test users and explicit constructions, not sleeps.
4. After the last package converts: if dbtest.Connect (the serial shared-DB path) has zero remaining callers, remove it and its advisory-lock plumbing in the SAME change (dead serialization code invites accidental resurrection); if callers remain, list them and why.

DEPENDS ON: winzy.ai-utzz (the pilot defines the pattern).
ACCEPTANCE: full go test -tags=integration -race ./... green 10x consecutive; full-gate wall-clock <=20s (report the per-package table: baseline -> post-A -> post-B -> post-C); zero tests deleted/weakened; every parallelism finding documented.
close_reason: Phase C rollout complete: every integration package parallel on per-test clones, serial Connect path deleted, 10/10 + 3/3 race-green, full gate ~9-11s

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
EXECUTION STEPS (PM dispatch, 2026-07-16 — the bead description's RULES are binding; read it + backend/README's 'Parallel integration tests' checklist first):
1. Baseline: run the full integration suite once (-race -count=1) and record the per-package table.
2. Convert ONE package at a time in this order: activity, challenges, notifications, social, export, events, health, httpserver, db, auth LAST (rule 3: token-rotation/rate-limit tests may assume ordering — fix with per-test users/explicit construction, never sleeps, never re-serialization). cmd/api only if its tests touch the DB.
3. Per package: apply the README checklist (ConnectParallel + t.Parallel on every integration test), run that package -race -count=1 3x, note wall-clock. Findings per rule 2 — fix isolation properly, document each.
4. After the last package: count remaining dbtest.Connect callers. Zero -> delete Connect + its advisory-lock/truncate plumbing in the same change; nonzero -> list callers + why.
5. Refresh the CI timing tripwire comment/threshold in .github/workflows/ci-go.yml + backend/README (they say 'refresh when Phase C lands' — this is Phase C landing; pick ~3x the new steady state).
6. Final gates: gofmt -l . empty; go vet -tags=integration ./...; go build ./...; full TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgres://winzy:winzy@localhost:5439/winzy?sslmode=disable go test -tags=integration -race -count=1 ./... TEN consecutive greens; report the baseline->post-C per-package table.
7. STOP and report on this thread PER-PACKAGE (files, findings, timings), then release reservations. Never commit/push/close; no br writes; nothing on port 5050/E2E without PM GO.

All remaining packages converted (auth's 99 tests included — zero ordering findings, no sleeps/re-serialization anywhere); dbtest.Connect had zero callers afterward and was deleted with its advisory-lock/truncate/migrate-once plumbing in the same change (ConnectParallel is the sole fixture); overhead measurement test rewritten for ConnectParallel behind DBTEST_MEASURE=1; CI tripwire refreshed 40s→33s (~3x the new ~11s steady state). Full-suite wall ~9-11s vs ~13s Phase A+B and ~300s pre-epic. Worker's 10/10 acceptance runs: 8.9-11.3s walls.

title: Close-out: CI parallel config, timing report, docs + skill updates
description:
CLOSE-OUT of winzy.ai-s8ly. Lock the wins in so they cannot silently regress, and update every place that teaches the old slow rules.

TASKS
1. ci-go.yml: confirm -p 1 removal (done in the per-package-DB bead), add a timing step: go test -json output piped to a tiny summarizer (a go run ./tools/... script or jq) printing a per-package duration table into the CI log; add a SOFT tripwire — warn (log annotation, not fail) when full integration exceeds 120s, so drift is visible before it hurts.
2. Pre-push hook (find it: .git/hooks or husky/lint-staged config at repo root): ensure it uses the parallel invocation, no stale -p 1.
3. backend/README Test section: final rewrite — the fast invocation, winzy_test_* database family, ConnectParallel pattern pointer, the production-Argon2 pin-test note, the fsync-off warning, and REMOVE stale -p 1 guidance.
4. .claude/skills/worker-dispatch/SKILL.md (repo) + ~/.claude/skills/worker-dispatch/SKILL.md (generalized): retire the integration-test PM GO protocol (per-package DBs made cross-agent integration runs collision-free); keep GO slots for: E2E runs (port 5050 + shared winzy DB via compose), parity runs (:5051/winzy_parity), migration rehearsals (winzy-mig-db). State this as a delta with the date.
5. CLAUDE.md Testing section: no change needed unless the three-areas rule text references timing; verify only.
6. Final epic report on winzy.ai-s8ly before closing it: the complete baseline->final timing table (unit + per-package integration + full gate), the phase-by-phase deltas, and the 3 rules that keep it fast (pin test, tripwire, parallel-by-default).

DEPENDS ON: per-package-DB bead (minimum); re-verify after the rollout bead lands and update the numbers.
ACCEPTANCE: CI shows the timing table + tripwire; all docs/skills updated with no stale -p 1 or GO-protocol references for integration tests; epic closed with the final table.
close_reason: testtiming tool (go test -json -> per-package table + WALL + soft GHA warning), CI piped through it with 40s tripwire (3x measured 13s steady state), README/skills finalized, no double-edits with edxi. PM independent gate: WALL 12.95s all green through the new tool. Worker: SageFrog.
