comments.text: FRESH-EYES NOTE (PM, 2026-07-12): task 2's pre-push hook hunt — start with test-backend.sh at the repo root and the actual git hook (.git/hooks/pre-push or husky config); test-backend.sh is also called out in winzy.ai-edxi step 5, coordinate so the -p 1 removal isn't done twice/divergently. Also: when re-verifying after winzy.ai-zfa3, refresh the CI tripwire threshold to match the post-Phase-C numbers (120s would be uselessly loose if the gate lands at ~15s — set warn threshold to ~3x the measured steady state).

title: Web serving: proper cache headers (no-cache index.html, immutable hashed assets)
description:
Found during the 2026-07-13 stale-dist incident: backend/internal/web/static.go serves index.html and assets via http.ServeFile/FileServer with NO Cache-Control headers — browsers fall back to heuristic caching and can keep an old index.html for a long time (observed: a reload kept a stale index.html referencing a March bundle; only a cache-busting URL fetched the new one). The old C# gateway had the same gap, so this is status-quo-equivalent, not a regression — but after cutover it slows how fast users pick up new web bundles, and the PWA shell makes it worse.

FIX (small, in web.SPAHandler / SplitHandler static branch):
1. index.html (and the SPA fallback response): Cache-Control: no-cache (forces revalidation; ETag/Last-Modified still make it a cheap 304).
2. /_expo/static/** (content-hashed filenames): Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable.
3. /assets/** (NOT content-hashed): Cache-Control: no-cache, same revalidation logic as index.html — do NOT mark immutable.
4. sw.js: Cache-Control: no-cache (service-worker updates depend on refetching it).

ACCEPTANCE: unit tests asserting the header per path class; curl verification against a local WEB_DIST serve; no behavior change for API routes. Sequencing: nice-to-have BEFORE cutover (rdc7.10) so the first Go deploy ships correct headers; not a blocker.
close_reason: Cache-Control per path class in web.SPAHandler: /_expo/static/** immutable 1y; index.html, sw.js, /assets/**, SPA fallback no-cache. Unit tests per class incl. missing-hashed-file fallback not immutable. gofmt/vet/build/tests green. Ships in the first Go deploy (rdc7.10).

title: Habits export: N+1 queries (2N+1 round trips) — batch with ANY(habit_ids)
description:
From the 2026-07-13 delegated export-module review (deep-explore agent; PM-triaged). backend/internal/habits/export.go:80-114 loops over the user's habits and issues TWO queries per habit (completionsForExport store.go:442, promisesForExport promise_store.go:220) = 2N+1 round trips. The C# original (habit-service InternalEndpoints.cs:19-23) fetched everything in ONE EF query with Include(h=>h.Completions) — so this is a query-shape regression vs the spec, not a port.

Aggravators (same review): habit count per user is uncapped (store.go createHabit has no limit), and backend/internal/db/db.go:16 builds the pgxpool with NO MaxConns override (small default). A user with thousands of habits ties one pool connection up for thousands of sequential round trips inside one export request (rate-limited 1/60s per user, so not a flood — an availability papercut, not a leak).

FIX: (a) batch-fetch completions and promises with WHERE habit_id = ANY($1::uuid[]) (one query each), group in memory per habit; (b) while in db.go, set an explicit pool MaxConns (document the chosen value); (c) keep export output byte-identical (goldens/parity pin it — rerun the export parity scenario after).
ACCEPTANCE: same export JSON (diff against a pre-change capture), query count per export = O(1) not O(N) (assert via a test that counts queries or by log inspection), gates green.
close_reason: Habits export batched: 2N+1 queries -> 3, ordering byte-identical (test-pinned); pool MaxConns pinned to 20

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: DONE (2026-07-16, Sonnet worker + PM line-read + independent -race -count=1 gates on habits/db/cmd-api). Export now does 3 queries total (habits list + batched completions + batched promises via ANY($1::uuid[])) instead of 2N+1; ORDER BY habit_id,<orig key> preserves the exact per-habit ordering (verified by a new interleaved-order integration test); zero-completion habits still marshal as [] (make([]T, len(nil)) is empty-non-nil). db.New now pins pool MaxConns=20 (documented: single instance, one shared pool, prevents one bulk request starving the rest). Worker worktree was 2 commits behind — 3-way apply clean.

title: Auth export: duplicate user fetch + delete-race yields misleading 200-with-warning instead of 404
description:
From the 2026-07-13 delegated export-module review (deep-explore agent; PM-triaged). backend/internal/auth/service.go:379-393 (Service.Export) fetches the user to gate on 404, DISCARDS the row, then the auth export section (service.go:409-426) fetches the SAME user again. Two issues: (1) a wasted duplicate query on every export; (2) TOCTOU — if DELETE /auth/account lands between the two fetches, the outer gate has already passed and the inner fetch returns ErrNotFound, which is not export.ErrNoData, so the registry (registry.go:88-94) degrades it to a warning: the caller receives HTTP 200 with a document MISSING the auth section, when the correct outcome (matching export_integration_test.go AfterAccountDeletedReturnsNotFound intent) is 404.

FIX: fetch once — pass the already-loaded user (or its data) into the auth section, or make Service.Export assemble the auth section from the single fetch before invoking the registry for the other modules. Keep response shape identical.
ACCEPTANCE: one user query per export (verifiable in code); a test simulating the deletion race (delete between gate and registry call) asserting 404 or a justified documented alternative; gates green.
close_reason: Auth export fetches the user once — TOCTOU delete-race eliminated by construction (auth section assembled from the gate fetch, no longer a registered Section); duplicate query gone

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: DONE (2026-07-16, Sonnet worker, 2 rounds + PM line-read + independent -race -count=1 gates). Round 1 (context-threading + defensive fallback) REJECTED by PM: hidden data flow through context + permanently-dead branch. Round 2 implements the bead's cleaner option: auth no longer self-registers in export.Registry; Service.Export builds the auth ServiceExport directly from its single existence-gate fetch (newAuthExportData(user) — pure, no ctx/DB) and prepends it, preserving the old response order. Race closed BY CONSTRUCTION (no second query exists); one user query per export, verifiable in code. Pin test asserts section order [auth, ...] + data from the gate fetch; sequential delete-then-export 404 stays covered by the existing HTTP-level test. Docs updated in export/registry.go + Export.

title: tools/migrate: real Railway targeting (--target-url, sslmode, owner fix)
description:
BLOCKS CUTOVER (rdc7.10). From the 2026-07-13 runbook fresh-eyes review (file-verified): tools/migrate cannot target Railway's Postgres today —
1. Target db name is the hardcoded constant TargetDB=\"winzy_rehearsal\" (internal/config/config.go:19,86-93); no flag changes it.
2. SourceURL/TargetURL hardcode sslmode=disable (config.go:86-89) — Railway's public proxy needs sslmode=require; make it a flag/env with a safe default for local.
3. restore.recreateDB runs CREATE DATABASE ... OWNER \"winzy\" using the CONSTANT DefaultUser, not cfg.User (internal/restore/restore.go:74) — fails on Railway unless a role literally named winzy exists.

FIX:
- Add --target-url (full override, wins over host/port/user/db composition) OR --target-db + --sslmode flags — pick the design that keeps the local rehearse one-command unchanged (defaults preserve current behavior exactly).
- recreateDB owner = cfg.User (or omit OWNER — creator owns by default).
- IMPORTANT SAFETY: the ForbiddenDBs guard (config.go:21) must still apply, and the tool must REFUSE to run restore/load against a target whose URL it cannot parse or that matches a forbidden name.
- Load/verify/authcheck/schema must all honor the same target override (they all use cfg.TargetURL()).
- README: document the Railway invocation (target-url from railway variables; NEVER commit URLs).

ACCEPTANCE: local `rehearse` unchanged (rerun it green against winzy-mig-db as the regression check — needs a PM GO for the DB); a dry-run/parse-only test proving a railway-style URL (sslmode=require, different db name, different user) composes correctly through all subcommands; gates green.
close_reason: Added target-url (full override), target-db, and sslmode flags across prepare-target/load/verify/authcheck; recreateDB owner fixed to cfg.User; ForbiddenDBs guard enforced on parsed targets. Local rehearse regression green (defaults byte-unchanged, all deltas +0); railway-style URL dry-run tests pass. Cutover blocker cleared. Worker: SageFrog.

title: Strip C#-compat shims from the Go backend (post-cutover greenfield cleanup)
description: Owner directive 2026-07-14: bug-for-bug parity with the old .NET stack is no longer a constraint — the old stack is deleted and the only client is our own frontend. Remove compat kept solely for parity, redesigning cleanly where it simplifies: RoundNET C#-rounding helper (use plain Go rounding), UTF-16 password-length semantics (plain len/rune count), exact legacy error-message strings (write clear messages; update frontend expectations in the same change), enum casing conversion shims where wire format can be normalized (coordinate with frontend types via openapi regen), legacy request shapes kept only because C# accepted them (e.g. string-enum inputs from h0ne — check frontend usage first). RULE: every removal ships together with the frontend change that consumes it; E2E green. Est. -500 to -1000 LOC. NOT included: anything the frontend actually depends on staying stable mid-change.
close_reason: C#-compat shims stripped: .NET rounding, UTF-16 lengths, dual-form enum inputs, export casing — one canonical wire form, frontend updated in same change, all gates + E2E green

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: DONE (2026-07-16). Removed: roundNET/RoundNET bit-exact .NET banker's rounding (replaced with plain round-half-away-from-zero: round1 in habits, math.Round inline in social + badge SVG), utf16Len/UTF16Len UTF-16 code-unit counting (replaced with utf8.RuneCountInString at all 6 sites: passwords, search query, reward description, witness label, private note), net_compat.go + its test deleted, dual-form enum inputs dropped (completionKind numeric+PascalCase, customDays day-name strings, milestoneType numeric ordinals — wire is now lowercase/camelCase strings + int ordinals only, matching what the frontend actually sends), case-insensitive Frequency/milestone parsing tightened to exact wire enum, export payload frequency normalized to lowercase (was PascalCase per C# quirk), CompletionKind got symmetric MarshalJSON. openapi.yaml: CompletionKindInput/dual CustomDayInput collapsed to canonical forms; frontend types regenerated; frontend now sends 'full'/'minimum' strings (COMPLETION_KIND numeric constant deleted, updateCompletion/useHabitDetail/HabitDetailScreen updated). KEPT deliberately: uuidValue/friendIDValue decoders (real request hygiene, friendlier field errors), Argon2id params (live constraint — migrated hashes), clear verbatim messages (they are good messages; deleted only the parity-harness comment claims). Net -370 LOC (bead estimated -500..-1000; the rest was the kept items). GATES: gofmt/vet clean, unit+integration+race green, frontend tsc+lint+1086 tests green, E2E vs rebuilt image 45 passed/2 skipped. User-visible change: consistency midpoints now round up (6.25% shows 6.3%, was 6.2%).

title: ffe8 follow-up: frontend still counted UTF-16 units against backend rune limits (gpt-sol review find)
description: Cross-model (gpt-5.6-sol) review of the ffe8 shim removal found the frontend half incomplete: backend moved to rune counting but frontend validation used JS .length (UTF-16 units) — password '123456😀' passed client validation and got rejected server-side; 300-emoji challenge rewards were blocked client-side though backend-valid. Also http/habits.http still sent day-name customDays the API now rejects. Fix: codePointLength helper in validation.ts used for password + challenge reward gate/hint; http collection uses int ordinals. Rounding findings from the same review (round1 binary-tie at e.g. 28.75) were assessed NOT-A-REGRESSION: the old bit-exact .NET rounding produced identical results on identical intermediates — inherent float behavior, no action.
close_reason: Fixed in the same sitting: codePointLength in validation.ts (password gate + tests), CreateChallengeScreen reward gate/hint, http/habits.http int ordinals; tsc + 1087 Jest tests green

title: pre-commit hook ignored lint-staged failures
description: Discovered 2026-07-16: .husky/pre-commit ran 'npx lint-staged' without checking its exit code, so a commit went through despite an eslint error (caught because the error was visible in output). Fixed by appending '|| exit 1'. Rides with the next commit.
close_reason: || exit 1 appended; verified lint failures now abort the commit

title: Browser back button closes the app instead of navigating back
description:
From GH issue #140 (deleted 2026-07-16). On web, pressing the browser/hardware back button exits the app instead of closing the current overlay or returning to the previous screen.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed by architecture research 2026-07-16): navigation is pure in-memory React state. frontend/src/navigation/useOverlayRouter.ts is a useReducer overlay stack; tab switching is setActiveTab in RootNavigator.tsx:87-122. The ONLY history API usage in the whole frontend is two window.history.replaceState calls (RootNavigator.tsx:168, :175) for clearing /@username and /w/{token} URLs. There is no popstate listener and no pushState anywhere — the browser history never gets an entry for any in-app navigation, so browser-back leaves the SPA entirely.

HISTORY MODEL (revised 2026-07-16 after PM review — the naive "push an entry per navigation" model creates back-button spam; this is the deliberate design):
- OVERLAYS: each overlay.push pushes exactly one history entry. popstate pops the top overlay. Closing an overlay via its own UI (X/done) calls history.back() and lets the popstate handler perform the state change — ONE code path for closing, whether via UI or browser back; this is what prevents stale/duplicate entries.
- TABS: do NOT push an entry per tab tap (10 tab taps must not mean 10 back presses to exit). Leaving the home tab (today) pushes ONE sentinel entry; subsequent tab switches replaceState on that sentinel; browser back from any non-today tab consumes the sentinel and returns to today; back on the base entry (today, no overlays) exits the app. This matches Android back conventions (back goes to the home tab, then exits).
- COMBINED: overlay entries stack on top of the current tab entry. Tab switch with overlays open (which calls overlay.closeAll) must first unwind the overlay entries (history.go(-depth)) before handling the tab sentinel — the popstate handler needs a suppress-counter to distinguish its own programmatic back operations from real user backs. This is the classic pitfall of history sync; implement it as a small self-contained module (e.g. src/navigation/useHistorySync.ts) with unit tests, NOT inline in RootNavigator.
- REFRESH mid-state: in-memory overlay stack is gone on reload — on boot, replaceState to a clean base entry so leftover entries from the previous page-load can't desync the counter.
- FUTURE-PROOF: the integration lives at the router level (useOverlayRouter/tab dispatch), so overlays added later (todos, meditation, challenge-invite wizard beads) inherit correct back behavior with zero extra work — this is a hard requirement, not an optimization.
- Native: guard all history usage behind Platform.OS === "web"; Android hardware back on the installed PWA flows through the same popstate path.
- Keep the special web-only routes working: /@username and /w/{token} are read from window.location.pathname (RootNavigator.tsx:48-73, 180-199) with replaceState clearing; the history-sync module must not fight this (initialize AFTER those branches resolve).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Overlay open -> browser back closes it, app stays alive; nested overlays close one per back.
- From Friends/Feed/Profile, back returns to Today; back on Today (no overlays) exits — and wandering across many tabs still needs only ONE back to reach Today.
- Overlay X button then browser back does NOT produce a dead click (no stale entry).
- Tab switch while overlays are open leaves history consistent (subsequent back behaves per model).
- /@username and /w/{token} public pages and their click-through-to-signup behavior unchanged.
- Unit tests for the history-sync module (suppress-counter, closeAll unwind, refresh re-init); Playwright E2E: open overlay -> page.goBack() -> overlay closed + app alive; switch to Friends -> page.goBack() -> Today. Frontend gates green.

KEY FILES: frontend/src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx, frontend/src/navigation/useOverlayRouter.ts, frontend/src/navigation/TabBar.tsx.
notes:
EXECUTION STEPS
1. Read the full spec: `br show winzy.ai-y4s1` (READ-ONLY — you never run br write commands). The description's HISTORY MODEL section is the design; follow it exactly.
2. Reserve frontend/src/navigation/** plus any e2e spec file you add (file_reservation_paths, ttl 7200, reason winzy.ai-y4s1).
3. Read RootNavigator.tsx, useOverlayRouter.ts, TabBar.tsx and the two replaceState sites before writing code.
4. Implement src/navigation/useHistorySync.ts as a self-contained module (overlay entries, single tab sentinel, suppress-counter, one close code path, boot replaceState, Platform web guards) and wire it at ROUTER level only.
5. Unit tests for the module (suppress-counter, closeAll unwind, refresh re-init). WRITE the two Playwright E2E scenarios but do NOT run the E2E suite (port-5050 lane needs a PM GO; frontend gates are enough for this round).
6. Gates from frontend/: npx tsc --noEmit && npm run lint && npm test. If a failure points at files you did NOT touch, that is another worker's in-flight edit — report it to the PM, do not fix it.
7. Report on this thread: files changed, design decisions, full gate output tails, deviations, open questions. Then release your reservations.
close_reason: History sync shipped, PM line-read + fix round (pending-merge), 70 nav tests, committed

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: Review policy (PM): the history-sync suppress-counter logic is subtle and a classic source of bugs — PM line-read plus grok review minimum; consider gpt-5.6-sol if the implementation turns out hairier than specced. Also coordinate with the notification-click bug (winzy.ai-penv): its routing must go through the same router-level APIs this bead formalizes.

title: Space key dismisses open dialogs on web
description:
From GH issue #124 (deleted 2026-07-16). On laptop/web, pressing the space bar closes some dialogs.

LIKELY MECHANISM (from architecture research 2026-07-16, needs a quick repro to confirm): frontend/src/design-system/components/Modal.tsx wraps RN Modal with a full-screen backdrop Pressable that has accessibilityRole="button" and onPress={onClose} (lines 25-30). On react-native-web that renders a focusable <div role="button" tabIndex=0>, and RNW fires onPress for Enter AND Space keydown on button-role elements. When focus lands on the backdrop (default focus on open, or via Tab), Space anywhere dismisses the dialog. Affected consumers include FlameIntroModal.tsx and ChallengeCompletionOverlay.tsx (both wrap this Modal). NOT affected: HabitListScreen archive confirm (native window.confirm) and HabitDetailScreen archive (no web dialog).

SPEC:
- Reproduce first (open FlameIntroModal on web, press Space) and confirm the mechanism before changing code.
- Make the backdrop non-keyboard-activatable: remove the button role / make it non-focusable (e.g. focusable={false} + accessibilityRole none or tabIndex -1 on web) while KEEPING click/tap-to-dismiss.
- Add proper Escape-to-close on web (keydown listener while the modal is mounted) so keyboard users still have a dismissal path — this replaces the accidental space behavior with the standard one.
- Ensure focus does not silently fall on any destructive/confirm button by default in these dialogs (space/enter on a genuinely focused button is correct behavior and must stay).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- With a Modal-based dialog open on web, pressing Space with no button focused does nothing; clicking the backdrop still closes; Escape closes.
- Tapping inside the dialog content still does not close it (existing stopPropagation behavior, Modal.tsx:33).
- Native (iOS/Android) behavior unchanged.
- Unit test for the modal keyboard behavior; frontend gates green.

KEY FILES: frontend/src/design-system/components/Modal.tsx, frontend/src/screens/FlameIntroModal.tsx, frontend/src/components/ChallengeCompletionOverlay.tsx.
notes:
EXECUTION STEPS
1. `br show winzy.ai-k1o9` (READ-ONLY — no br write commands ever).
2. Reserve frontend/src/design-system/components/Modal.tsx + its test file + any consumer you must touch (reason winzy.ai-k1o9).
3. REPRODUCE FIRST on web (npm run web or the dev server; open FlameIntroModal or ChallengeCompletionOverlay, press Space) and document the repro + confirmed mechanism in your report before fixing.
4. Fix: backdrop must not be keyboard-activatable (keep click/tap dismiss); add Escape-to-close on web while mounted; ensure no destructive button holds default focus.
5. Unit tests for the keyboard behavior (Space inert, Escape closes, backdrop click closes, inner tap does not).
6. Gates from frontend/: npx tsc --noEmit && npm run lint && npm test. Failures in files you didn't touch → report, don't fix.
7. Report on this thread (files, decisions, gate tails, deviations), then release reservations.
close_reason: Fixed, reviewed (repro-confirmed mechanism, independent gates), committed ed5abb2

title: Evaluate 'My Habits' screen redundancy and propose consolidation
description:
From GH issue #127 (deleted 2026-07-16): "the my habits page seems redundant". Gabriel chose INVESTIGATE AND PROPOSE FIRST — this bead is analysis only, no implementation. Deliverable is a written proposal for Gabriel to pick a direction; implementation beads get created after his sign-off.

CURRENT FACTS (architecture research 2026-07-16):
- HabitListScreen.tsx is the ONLY place to create, edit, and archive habits (edit opens CreateHabitScreen pre-filled; archive via long-press or per-row Archive button). It shows frequency + visibility badge but no flame/completion state — pure CRUD management.
- It is an overlay, NOT a tab: reachable ONLY via the "+ Create Habit" FAB on TodayScreen (overlay.push("habits"), RootNavigator.tsx:316-333). A returning user who wants to edit/archive must go through a FAB labeled "create".
- TodayScreen shows due-today habits with completion toggles + flame; HabitDetailScreen has the calendar, stats, and per-habit actions.

THE PROPOSAL SHOULD COVER:
- What is genuinely redundant vs what is the only home of a function (create/edit/archive).
- At least two concrete options with tradeoffs, e.g. (a) kill the screen: FAB becomes pure create, edit/archive move into HabitDetailScreen, Today gains an "all habits incl. not-due-today" affordance; (b) keep but promote: make it a real management view (rename, clear entry point from Profile/Settings, and future home of reordering — see the habit-reordering bead, which needs a management surface).
- Interaction with planned work: habit reordering needs a place to live; the Today week-strip changes Today's density.
- A recommendation.

title: Verify push notifications end-to-end on staging
description:
From GH issue #134 (deleted 2026-07-16): "Make sure notifications work. I haven't even tested if they work." P1: push delivery is the core promise of the social layer and it has never been verified against real browsers. Deliverable is a written report; defects become beads.

WHAT EXISTS (research 2026-07-16): web push via github.com/marknefedov/go-webpush/v2; VAPID keys from Railway env (continuity-critical, do NOT rotate); delivery in a detached goroutine after commit (notifications/push.go:206-283); push-triggering events: HabitCompleted (fan-out to friends with FriendActivity on), FriendRequestSent/Accepted, ChallengeCreated/Completed. The pipeline is tested against a fake PushSender only — never a real browser. Frontend: sw.js handles push + notificationclick; subscribe flow in frontend/src/utils/push.ts + usePushNotifications.ts; device registration via POST /notifications/devices.

ALREADY-KNOWN GAPS — PRE-FILED, do NOT re-file, just confirm behavior while testing:
- habit_reminders is a dead settings flag (no scheduler exists) -> feature epic winzy.ai (see "Habit reminder notifications" epic).
- Notification CLICKS navigate nowhere (sw.js posts NOTIFICATION_CLICK, no app listener) -> pre-filed bug bead (see "Push notification clicks don't navigate"). Verify and note actual behavior in that bead.
- Expo/native push is an explicit stub (push.go:168-171; frontend reports unsupported) — expected until native apps exist; just note it in the report.

VERIFY ON STAGING (Railway winzy-staging, real browsers, two test accounts that are friends with FriendActivity on):
1. Subscribe from the app UI on desktop Chrome (find the actual subscribe surface — likely SettingsScreen via usePushNotifications); confirm the device row lands in device_tokens with platform web_push.
2. Account A completes a habit -> account B receives the push; check title/body content quality (is the copy sensible?).
3. Friend request sent + accepted pushes; challenge created + completed pushes.
4. Permission-denied path: deny browser permission, confirm the UI degrades sanely (status "denied", no crash, no retry loop).
5. Mobile: Android Chrome installed-PWA delivery (background + closed); iOS Safari home-screen PWA (16.4+) — document results per platform honestly, including failures.
6. Unsubscribe: toggle off -> no further deliveries; verify token cleanup server-side (or document that stale tokens accumulate and how 410-gone responses are handled by the sender — check push.go's failure handling while in there).
7. Settings toggles actually gate delivery: turn FriendActivity off on B -> A's habit completion sends nothing.

ACCEPTANCE: report as a comment on THIS bead covering every numbered check with pass/fail + environment details; follow-up beads filed for each NEW defect (beyond the pre-filed ones); the two pre-filed beads updated with confirmed behavior.
notes:
EXECUTION STEPS
1. `br show winzy.ai-i1q6` (READ-ONLY — no br write commands ever).
2. ZERO repo file edits — the working tree belongs to code workers. Scratch scripts go under /tmp, never the repo. Nothing to reserve.
3. Target: https://api-gateway-production-9faf.up.railway.app — this IS the live deployment (winzy.ai is a parked third-party domain, do not use it). Create two clearly-marked test accounts (e.g. pushtest-a / pushtest-b), make them friends, FriendActivity on.
4. Desktop verification: headed Chromium (Playwright is fine) with notification permission granted for the origin; work through checks 1-7 in the spec — real subscribe, real event triggers from the other account, real push delivery observed. Inspect device_tokens indirectly via behavior (you have no DB access to prod — infer from API responses and delivery).
5. Anything not verifiable from this machine gets marked exactly that, with the reason. Produce the 5-minute OWNER CHECKLIST section for the mobile checks (Android Chrome PWA, iOS 16.4+ home-screen PWA).
6. Report on this thread: per-check pass/fail + environment details + confirmed live behavior relevant to winzy.ai-wyw6 (reminders) and winzy.ai-penv (click routing) — the PM transfers those notes to the beads, you do not.
7. Clean up: delete or clearly abandon the test accounts' subscriptions (unsubscribe) at the end; report what test data remains on prod.
close_reason: Desktop verification complete: 7/7 checks pass on live deployment; gaps confirmed and filed (wyw6, penv, new DisplayName bead); mobile = optional owner checklist in the report comment

title: Research: home screen widgets feasibility (native + PWA interim)
description:
From GH issue #133 (deleted 2026-07-16): "Add home screen widgets? Is that possible?" Research bead — deliverable is a feasibility report + effort estimate + follow-up beads if viable. No implementation.

CONTEXT: Winzy is a web-first Expo PWA; real home-screen widgets require native app targets (iOS WidgetKit / Android AppWidget-Glance). Current PWA has NO badging, NO manifest shortcuts, no background/periodic sync (research 2026-07-16 — manifest.json + sw.js confirmed bare of these).

INVESTIGATE (verify current docs via Context7/web — do not trust memory):
1. Native path: expo-apple-targets (or current equivalent) for WidgetKit widgets; Android Glance/AppWidget with Expo — what each requires (EAS build, dev-client, config plugins), whether it works without ejecting, and what data a widget could show (today's habits + completion state would need an app-group/shared-storage bridge or a small widget API endpoint).
2. What the WEB app can offer meanwhile: Badging API (navigator.setAppBadge) support matrix for installed PWAs (Chrome/Android, Safari/iOS) — e.g. badge count of habits not yet logged today; manifest shortcuts (long-press app icon quick actions like "Log today"); anything else current (e.g. iOS lock-screen web-app capabilities as of 2026).
3. Effort estimate for each option and prerequisites (native builds pipeline does not exist yet — that is the real cost of the native path).

ACCEPTANCE: report as comment on this bead (options, support matrices, effort, recommendation) + follow-up beads for whatever Gabriel picks. Explicitly answer the original question: "is that possible?"
close_reason: Research complete, report in comment: real widgets impossible for a PWA (native-only OS surfaces); native path deferred until a native-apps decision exists; cheap PWA layer (badge + shortcuts) filed as winzy.ai-1vyl

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text:
RESEARCH REPORT (native Sonnet agent, 2026-07-17) — answering "home screen widgets, is that possible?"

SHORT ANSWER: Not as a PWA — OS widgets (iOS WidgetKit / Android AppWidget) can only attach to an installed NATIVE app package; an installed PWA is a manifest bookmark and cannot host a widget extension. True widgets are gated on Winzy someday shipping native builds (EAS, Apple $99/yr + Play $25 accounts, store records, TestFlight/internal testing — a multi-week infra effort and a strategy reversal from web-first). If/when native happens: Expo SDK 57 (2026-06) ships an OFFICIAL expo-widgets for iOS WidgetKit (no Swift needed, app-group data bridge, ~1-2 weeks on top of native infra); Android has community-only tooling (react-native-android-widget / Glance, real Kotlin, ~2-3 weeks, more risk). Total native path ~2-3 months elapsed incl. Phase-0 infra.

WHAT THE PWA CAN DO TODAY (2026 status):
- Badging: iOS Safari 16.4+ supports navigator.setAppBadge for installed PWAs (needs notification permission; EU/DMA behavior flaky — test on real EU devices). Android Chrome does NOT implement the API but AUTO-BADGES the PWA icon on unread push — meaning our existing web push already produces Android badges for free.
- Manifest shortcuts (long-press icon quick actions): Android/Chrome yes, iOS no.
- That is the full extent of installed-PWA glanceability; no web content can occupy a real widget surface on either OS.

RECOMMENDATION (adopted): ship the cheap PWA layer (badge count of habits not yet logged + manifest shortcuts) — filed as a follow-up bead; do NOT pursue native widgets as a standalone initiative — they are a downstream benefit of a future native-apps decision, not a reason to make it.

Full report with sources preserved in the PM session log 2026-07-17 (MDN Badging API, docs.expo.dev/versions/latest/sdk/widgets, expo SDK 57 changelog, react-native-android-widget docs, web.dev PWA guides).

title: Expanded emoji picker for habit icons (more choice + free input)
description:
From GH issue #128 (deleted 2026-07-16): "Option to select more or custom emojis? the selection is very limited."

CURRENT STATE (research 2026-07-16): HABIT_ICONS is a hardcoded 20-emoji array local to frontend/src/screens/CreateHabitScreen.tsx:23-28, rendered as a radio row (lines 351-366). The chosen emoji is stored as a plain string in habit.icon. Backend accepts any string (habits/validation.go:40-45 has no icon validation; DB column varchar(64)).

SPEC (frontend-only):
- EXTRACT the icon picker into a shared component (e.g. design-system or components/IconPicker) — the challenge-invite creator wizard (winzy.ai-jc38.3) needs the same picker for its habit proposal step; CreateHabitScreen and that wizard must share one implementation, not fork it.
- The shared picker offers a substantially larger curated set (~80-120 emoji) organized by category (health/fitness, mind, food, learning, home, social, misc) in a scrollable grid — collapsed to one sensible row by default so CreateHabitScreen does not grow for users who do not care.
- "Custom" affordance: a small input accepting ANY emoji typed/pasted via the OS emoji keyboard. Validate single grapheme cluster (Intl.Segmenter granularity "grapheme" — verify current RN-web + Hermes support via Context7 before relying on it); reject plain text/multiple graphemes with a friendly message.
- Edit mode: a custom emoji not in the curated set must render as the selected value when editing an existing habit.
- Defensive client-side length cap (icon is varchar(64); any single emoji ZWJ sequence fits).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Pick from the expanded set; enter an arbitrary emoji; both save and render everywhere icons appear (Today rows, HabitDetail, public flame page).
- Existing habits with legacy icons unaffected; edit shows current icon selected.
- Non-emoji and multi-grapheme input rejected with a clear message.
- Component tests for grapheme validation + selection + collapsed/expanded states; frontend gates green.

NON-GOALS: backend validation changes. A third-party emoji-picker dependency is allowed ONLY if it cleanly supports RN-web + native and is actively maintained (verify via Context7); otherwise build the simple grid ourselves.
notes:
EXECUTION STEPS
1. `br show winzy.ai-zplo` (READ-ONLY, no br writes). Spec = description.
2. Reserve frontend/src/screens/CreateHabitScreen.tsx + the new shared IconPicker component location (design-system or components — match where similar shared pieces live; check both before deciding) + tests.
3. EXTRACT the picker into the shared component FIRST (the challenge-invite wizard will reuse it later — one implementation, never a fork), then expand: ~80-120 curated emoji in categories, collapsed-by-default first row, custom input accepting any single emoji.
4. Grapheme validation: verify Intl.Segmenter support on RN-web + Hermes via Context7 BEFORE relying on it (fallback strategy if Hermes lacks it: accept the string if it's <=8 UTF-16 code units and passes an emoji-ish pattern — document whichever path you take).
5. Edit mode: a custom emoji not in the curated set renders as selected when editing.
6. Third-party picker dependency ONLY if it cleanly supports RN-web + native and is maintained (Context7-verify) — otherwise build the simple grid (preferred).
7. Component tests (validation, selection, collapsed/expanded, edit-mode custom). Full frontend gates; failures in files you didn't touch -> report, don't fix.

title: Completions range endpoint (week view backend)
description:
From GH issue #126 (deleted 2026-07-16) — backend half of "see and check the whole current week from the main page".

CURRENT STATE: GET /habits/completions?date=YYYY-MM-DD exists (habits/routes.go:45, handlers.go:298-313, service.go:486-514, store.go:280-317) but takes exactly ONE date and LEFT JOINs all active habits against that day. A 7-day strip would need 7 calls. Per-habit stats (GET /habits/{id}/stats) returns full completedDates history but requires one call per habit.

CONTRACT DECISION (revised 2026-07-16 — greenfield rule: design clean, never preserve legacy quirks): first, grep the frontend for actual usage of GET /habits/completions?date=. The architecture research found no frontend caller (TodayScreen derives completedToday from per-habit stats). If it is indeed unused, REPLACE the single-date contract outright: from/to become the required params, the date param and its handler branch are deleted, openapi updated — no dual-shape endpoint kept for a client that never existed. If a real caller turns up, migrate it in the same change (types regenerate anyway). Do not ship both shapes.

SPEC:
- GET /habits/completions?from=YYYY-MM-DD&to=YYYY-MM-DD. Errors (habits module convention, flat {"error": ...}, first failure wins): missing/malformed params, from > to, range longer than 31 days -> 400; unauthenticated -> 401.
- Response: {"from": ..., "to": ..., "habits": [{id, name, icon, color, frequency, customDays, minimumDescription, days: [{date, completed, completionKind}]}]} — one entry per ACTIVE habit, days covering EVERY date in the range (completed=false entries included so clients never compute gaps). frequency + customDays included so the client can render not-due days without extra calls.
- ONE SQL query for the whole range (generate_series over the date range cross-joined with the user's active habits, LEFT JOIN completions), not N queries and not per-day loops.
- Ordering: habits in list order (position once the reorder bead lands, created_at until then — do not duplicate ordering logic; reuse the same ORDER BY the list endpoint uses).
- openapi.yaml + npm run generate:api-types.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Integration tests (ConnectParallel, t.Parallel): happy (multi-habit multi-day matrix correct incl. minimum vs full kinds), edge (no habits -> empty array, 1-day range, 31-day boundary, habit archived mid-range excluded entirely, completions outside range excluded), error (each 400 case above, unauthenticated).
- If the old date= form was removed: grep proves no remaining reference in backend, frontend, e2e, or http/ collections (update http/habits.http).
- Backend gates green.
notes:
EXECUTION STEPS
1. `br show winzy.ai-hj82` (READ-ONLY — no br write commands ever).
2. FIRST: grep the frontend for any usage of /habits/completions with date= and report the finding — it decides replace-clean vs migrate-caller per the CONTRACT DECISION in the spec.
3. Reserve backend/internal/habits/** and http/habits.http. Reserve backend/openapi.yaml + the generated frontend types file ONLY while actually editing them, release quickly (another backend worker shares those files) — if the reservation conflicts, coordinate via the PM.
4. Implement the range endpoint per spec: one SQL query (generate_series LEFT JOIN), list-consistent ordering, flat error convention.
5. openapi.yaml + npm run generate:api-types at the END (after openapi is final). Update http/habits.http.
6. Test env: TEST_DATABASE_URL='postgres://winzy:winzy@localhost:5439/winzy?sslmode=disable' (container is up). During development use PACKAGE-SCOPED checks (go test -tags=integration -race ./internal/habits/...). Full gates (gofmt -l . empty, go vet -tags=integration ./..., go build ./..., full go test -tags=integration -race ./...) only at the END — if full-tree failures come from packages you did not touch, that is the other backend worker's WIP: report, don't fix.
7. Report on this thread (usage-grep finding, files, decisions, gate tails, deviations), then release reservations.
close_reason: Range endpoint shipped replace-clean, fix round (id tiebreak), independent -race gates green, committed

title: Week strip on Today screen — see and toggle the last 7 days
description:
From GH issue #126 (deleted 2026-07-16) — frontend half: "Make last couple of days visible and checkable from the main page ... so that I don't need to click on each habit."

DEPENDS ON: the completions range endpoint (winzy.ai-hj82) AND TanStack habits migration (winzy.ai-d56m.1 — this UI is habit-data-layer work; building it pre-TanStack means immediate rework, so it waits for the pattern-setter).

CURRENT STATE: TodayScreen (frontend/src/screens/TodayScreen.tsx) renders per-habit rows with a today-only toggle; past days are only reachable via HabitDetailScreen's month calendar, which cycles a tapped in-window day none -> full -> minimum -> none (HabitDetailScreen.tsx:311-377).

SPEC:
- Each habit row on Today gains a compact 7-day strip (today rightmost + 6 previous days), fed by ONE range-endpoint query (from=today-6, to=today) shared across all habits via TanStack (['completions', from, to]).
- SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH: the strip's today cell and the row's big today-toggle read the same cache entry and share the same mutation — toggling either updates both instantly. The strip does NOT derive today's state from the per-habit stats call.
- TAP SAFETY (revised 2026-07-16 — tiny dots in a scrollable list invite accidental edits of history): every day cell keeps a >=44px hit target even if the visual dot is small; a tap on a PAST day applies optimistically AND surfaces an undo affordance (snackbar/toast "Marked Tuesday — Undo", reuse the design system's toast if one exists, else a minimal inline undo chip). Today's cell needs no undo (it's the primary action and visibly togglable). No confirm dialogs — undo, not friction (encourage, never punish).
- Cycle semantics identical to the HabitDetail calendar: none -> full -> minimum (only if the habit has minimumDescription) -> none. EXTRACT the cycle logic shared with useHabitDetail's useToggleCompletion into one place rather than duplicating it.
- Visual restraint (explicit user constraint — the UI must not get uglier): small dots/squares with weekday initials, muted styling for not-due days (frequency/customDays come in the range response), disabled future days, minimal row-height growth. The strip must not visually compete with the main toggle.
- 60-day rolling-window guard shared with HabitDetail (irrelevant for 7 days but keep the guard in the shared logic).
- After a past-day toggle, invalidate that habit's stats so the flame updates without refresh.
- COORDINATION: Today is also gaining a to-dos section (winzy.ai-30sf.2) and a meditation header icon (winzy.ai-1put.1). This strip is the densest addition — whichever of the three lands later re-evaluates overall spacing with the others present.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Whole current week visible + toggleable per habit from Today without opening any habit; exactly one network call for the strip data.
- Toggling yesterday updates the flame without refresh; undo reverts both UI and server state.
- Not-due days muted; future days disabled; today's cell and big toggle never disagree.
- Unit tests for the shared cycle logic + undo revert; Playwright E2E: toggle a past day from Today, assert persisted after reload. Frontend gates green.
notes:
EXECUTION STEPS
1. `br show winzy.ai-rs2n` (READ-ONLY, no br writes). Spec = description; both dependencies are committed (range endpoint 4f83d07, TanStack habits c1b120c — queryKeys.completions.range(from,to) factory already exists, unwired, waiting for you).
2. You OWN frontend/src/screens/TodayScreen.tsx this round — reserve it + the new week-strip component + the hook/file you add for the range query + any shared cycle-logic extraction.
3. ONE range query for all habits: from=today-6 to=today via the completions.range key; today's cell and the big toggle must read/write the SAME cache (single source of truth — study useTodayHabits' toggle mutation first and reuse/extend it, do not fork completion logic).
4. Cycle semantics: extract the none->full->minimum(->none) cycle shared with useHabitDetail's toggle into one place; keep the 60-day window guard in the shared logic.
5. TAP SAFETY per spec: >=44px hit targets, undo affordance on past-day taps (reuse an existing toast/snackbar if the design system has one — check first; else a minimal inline undo chip), no confirm dialogs.
6. Visual restraint per spec: dots + weekday initials, muted not-due days (frequency/customDays come in the range response), disabled future days, minimal row growth.
7. After a past-day toggle: invalidate that habit's stats key (flame refresh).
8. Component tests (shared cycle logic, undo revert, strip rendering rules) + WRITE the Playwright E2E scenario (do not run — PM GO lane). Full frontend gates.
9. Report + release. NOTE: two siblings are migrating other domains concurrently — if gates surface failures in friends/feed/challenges files you didn't touch, report, don't fix.

title: Habit ordering: position column + reorder endpoint
description:
From GH issue #137 (deleted 2026-07-16) — backend half of "make reordering of habits possible, without accidental reorders or uglier UI".

CURRENT STATE: habits table (migrations/0003_habits.up.sql:18-30) has NO position column; list is ORDER BY created_at ASC (habits/store.go:80-103).

SPEC:
- New migration (next free number): ALTER TABLE habits ADD COLUMN position integer; backfill position = row_number() per user ordered by created_at (ALL habits including archived, 0-based); then SET NOT NULL. No DB default — the insert path computes it. Matching .down.sql.
- listHabits orders by position ASC, created_at ASC (deterministic tiebreak). VERIFY every other habit-listing path inherits the same order — HabitsForUser (feeds witness views), the public flame page query, and export — ordering is a user-visible product decision and must be consistent on every surface.
- Create path: new habit appended — position = COALESCE(MAX(position)+1, 0) for that user, computed inside the INSERT statement (single statement, no read-then-write race).
- New endpoint PUT /habits/order, body {"habitIds": [...]}: must be EXACTLY the caller's active (non-archived) habit IDs — no omissions, no extras, no duplicates, no other users' or unknown IDs. Any mismatch -> 400 {"error": ...} (habits convention). Applies positions 0..n-1 in one transaction. 401 unauthenticated. Archived habits keep their stale position (harmless; they rejoin at their old slot if ever unarchived — acceptable, document in code).
- openapi.yaml + npm run generate:api-types.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Integration tests (ConnectParallel): happy (reorder persists; list + range-completions + public flame surfaces all reflect it), edge (single habit; archive then reorder remaining; create-after-reorder appends at end; two concurrent reorders -> last write wins, no constraint errors), error (missing/extra/duplicate/foreign IDs -> 400, unauthenticated).
- Migration backfill test: pre-existing rows get deterministic created_at-ordered positions.
- Backend gates green.
notes:
EXECUTION STEPS
1. `br show winzy.ai-mtje` (READ-ONLY — no br write commands). Description = spec.
2. Reserve backend/internal/habits/** and backend/migrations/0010_habit_positions.*; reserve backend/openapi/openapi.yaml + frontend/src/api/generated/schema.ts ONLY while editing (mfns.1 worker shares them; coordinate via PM on conflict).
3. Migration number is 0010 (0009 = todos, committed). Backfill: position = row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY created_at, id) - 1 for ALL habits incl. archived; then SET NOT NULL. Down migration drops the column.
4. Ordering NOTE (supersedes the spec's pre-tiebreak wording): hj82 just added an id tiebreak — the new canonical order is position ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC. Apply it to listHabits AND completionsInRange (they are in deliberate lockstep — see the code comment) AND verify HabitsForUser/public-flame/export paths per the spec.
5. Create path: position computed inside the INSERT (COALESCE(MAX+1) pattern — internal/todos/store.go:createTodo is the committed reference).
6. PUT /habits/order per spec: EXACT active set, any mismatch -> 400 (habits churn slowly — no 409 tier here, unlike todos). One transaction (todos OrderTodos is the committed tx reference).
7. openapi.yaml + npm run generate:api-types at the END.
8. Package-scoped tests during dev (TEST_DATABASE_URL='postgres://winzy:winzy@localhost:5439/winzy?sslmode=disable'); full gates at end; failures from packages you didn't touch -> report, don't fix.
9. Report on this thread, release reservations.

title: Reorder mode in habit management UI
description:
From GH issue #137 (deleted 2026-07-16) — frontend half. Gabriel's constraints: reordering must not happen ACCIDENTALLY and must not make the UI UGLIER.

DEPENDS ON: the position/reorder-endpoint bead (winzy.ai-mtje), the My-Habits consolidation bead (the 9igm decision killed HabitListScreen — reorder now lives on TODAY), and TanStack habits migration (winzy.ai-d56m.1).

DESIGN (explicit mode on Today, not always-on drag):
- Today gets a quiet overflow affordance (e.g. a small "⋯" or long-press on the list header) with a "Reorder" action. Entering reorder mode: completion toggles and week strips hide, ALL active habits show as plain rows (due-today AND the "Not today" group merge into one flat orderable list), each with a drag handle or up/down chevrons; "Done" commits, "Cancel" reverts.
- NO reorder affordances outside this mode. This satisfies the no-accidents constraint.
- Persist on Done via PUT /habits/order (full active-ID list per the backend contract); optimistic order with revert + toast on failure; invalidate the habits query so Today re-renders in new order (due-today filtering reapplies on exit from reorder mode).
- Drag implementation: verify current RN-web + native drag options via Context7 (react-native-draggable-flatlist web support is historically poor). If nothing cleanly supports web drag, ship chevrons only — one-slot-per-tap is acceptable v1 and zero-dependency. Do NOT add a heavy dependency for marginal polish.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Order changed in reorder mode persists across reload and shows on Today (due-today section AND Not-today group each in saved relative order) and on the public flame page (same order everywhere).
- Impossible to trigger a reorder outside reorder mode; Cancel restores the pre-mode order exactly.
- Normal Today UI is visually identical except the single quiet affordance.
- Component tests for mode toggle + move + commit + cancel + merged-list ordering; frontend gates green.

title: Adopt TanStack Query for server-state caching
description:
From GH issue #129 (deleted 2026-07-16): "Add caching or something. For example react query or tanstack query so that everything is a bit snappier."

CURRENT STATE (research 2026-07-16): zero caching/data library — every screen hand-rolls useState + useEffect fetches with manual loading/error state (frontend/src/hooks/). fetchHabits() is called independently by useTodayHabits.ts:74 AND useHabits.ts:29 with zero sharing; cross-screen freshness is glued together with ad-hoc callback props (onHabitCreated etc. in RootNavigator.tsx:321-329). Only React Context is AuthContext. Separately, Today fires N+1 stats requests — that half of the snappiness problem is the batch-stats bead (winzy.ai-sp8f).

GOAL: TanStack Query as the single server-state layer — instant cached renders on revisit, background refetch, mutation-driven invalidation, deletion of the callback-prop plumbing.

STRUCTURE (3 children):
- .1 install + provider + query-key conventions + habits domain (THE PATTERN-SETTER — it now BLOCKS the week-strip UI (rs2n), reorder UI (6dj1), and todos UI (30sf.2), so schedule it early; everything else frontend builds on its conventions).
- .2 social/friends/feed/witness domains (parallel with .3 after .1).
- .3 challenges/notifications/stats/settings domains (parallel with .2 after .1).
The final child to merge of (.2/.3) runs the full Playwright E2E suite once, with both migrations in.

Verify current TanStack Query APIs via Context7 before implementing (v5+ APIs differ meaningfully from older memory).

DONE WHEN: no hand-rolled fetch-on-mount hooks remain for server data; creating/archiving/completing anything updates every visible screen without manual callbacks; frontend gates green; E2E green.

comments.author: gabko14
comments.text: All three children committed (c1b120c, d2f8333, + d56m.3). Epic stays open pending its DONE-WHEN E2E clause — full Playwright suite runs at the wave boundary (after the week strip lands), then this closes.

title: TanStack Query: install, provider, migrate habits domain
description:
Child 1 of the TanStack Query epic — the pattern-setter.

SPEC:
- Add @tanstack/react-query (verify current major + APIs via Context7; check RN/Expo-web notes — focusManager/onlineManager wiring for RN if applicable). QueryClientProvider at the app root (App.tsx, alongside AuthContext). Sensible defaults: staleTime ~30s, retry 1, refetchOnWindowFocus on for web.
- Design and DOCUMENT the query-key scheme in one place (e.g. frontend/src/api/queryKeys.ts): ['habits'], ['habit', id], ['habit', id, 'stats', timezone], ['completions', from, to]. Keys must be exported constants/factories, never inline strings.
- Migrate the habits domain hooks: useTodayHabits, useHabits, useHabitDetail (and the completions/stats fetching inside them) to useQuery/useMutation. Preserve the optimistic completion-toggle UX (onMutate snapshot + rollback onError + invalidate onSettled).
- Mutations (create/edit/archive habit, complete/uncomplete) invalidate the right keys so TodayScreen, HabitListScreen, and HabitDetailScreen stay consistent WITHOUT the callback props — delete onHabitCreated-style plumbing through RootNavigator for the habits domain.
- Auth integration: on logout, queryClient.clear() (no cross-account cache bleed).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Create a habit in the overlay -> Today updates without manual callback. Archive -> disappears everywhere. Toggle completion -> optimistic, survives error with rollback.
- Revisiting a screen renders instantly from cache then background-refetches.
- No fetchHabits duplication: exactly one queryFn for the habits list.
- Existing tests updated (wrap renders in a QueryClientProvider test util); frontend gates green (tsc, lint, npm test).
notes:
EXECUTION STEPS
1. `br show winzy.ai-d56m.1` AND `br show winzy.ai-d56m` (epic; READ-ONLY, no br writes). You are the PATTERN-SETTER — two sibling beads will copy your conventions verbatim.
2. Your territory: the whole frontend EXCEPT frontend/src/api/generated/ (regenerated by backend workers — never hand-edit). Reserve frontend/src/hooks/**, frontend/src/api/queryKeys.ts (new), frontend/App.tsx, package.json.
3. FIRST verify current TanStack Query v5 APIs via Context7 (QueryClientProvider, useQuery/useMutation object syntax, onMutate/onError/onSettled optimistic pattern, queryClient.clear) — do not code from memory.
4. npm install @tanstack/react-query (latest v5.x). Provider at the root alongside AuthContext; defaults: staleTime ~30s, retry 1, refetchOnWindowFocus true.
5. Key factories in ONE module (frontend/src/api/queryKeys.ts): habits.list, habit(id), habitStats(id, tz), completionsRange(from, to) — the range key matches the NEW GET /habits/completions?from=&to= endpoint (already committed; types in generated schema). Export factories; no inline keys anywhere.
6. Migrate the habits domain per spec: useTodayHabits, useHabits, useHabitDetail; optimistic completion toggle (snapshot/rollback/invalidate); mutations invalidate precisely; DELETE the onHabitCreated-style callback plumbing through RootNavigator for the habits domain; queryClient.clear() on logout.
7. Keep the N-per-habit stats fetching BEHAVIOR for now (a batch endpoint lands later as winzy.ai-sp8f) — migrate the pattern, not the request shape.
8. Tests: add a renderWithQueryClient test util; update all affected suites. Full frontend gates (tsc, lint, npm test).
9. Report on this thread: files, the exact key-factory + mutation conventions you established (the siblings will quote them), deviations. Release reservations.

title: Profile images
description:
From GH issue #135 (deleted 2026-07-16): "Add profile images".

CURRENT STATE (research 2026-07-16): users.avatar_url varchar(512) exists in the DB (migrations/0002_auth.up.sql) and UpdateProfileRequest accepts avatarUrl as a string — but NOTHING uses it: every avatar in the app is initials-only via design-system Avatar.tsx (8 usage sites), no upload endpoint, no blob storage, no image library anywhere.

DESIGN DECISIONS (made 2026-07-16, PM):
- Storage: in Postgres (bytea, one row per user), served by our own endpoint. At this scale (correctness > infra) an object store is unjustified overhead; Railway has no free blob store wired up. Client resizes to 512x512 before upload so rows stay ~<150KB. Revisit only if image traffic ever matters.
- The avatar serving endpoint is PUBLIC (allowlisted): avatars appear on the public flame page /@username, which is unauthenticated by design.
- users.avatar_url stores our serving path (single source of truth for "has an avatar").

Two children: backend (upload/serve/delete + GDPR) then frontend (picker + display). Backend first.

Frontend child .2 touches profile data fetching across many screens — if the TanStack social migration (winzy.ai-d56m.2) is done first, build on it; not a hard dependency.

title: Avatar upload, serving, and deletion endpoints
description:
Backend child of the Profile images epic.

SPEC:
- Migration (next number): user_avatars table — user_id uuid PK, data bytea NOT NULL, content_type varchar(64) NOT NULL, updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL. Lives in the auth module (it owns users).
- PUT /auth/avatar (authed): raw image body with Content-Type image/jpeg|image/png|image/webp; max 512KB (hard 413/400 per auth module conventions — pick 400 with {"errors":{"avatar":[...]}} auth-style validation). MUST decode server-side (image.Decode / golang.org/x/image/webp — verify current lib via Context7) to prove it is a real image and check dimensions <= 1024x1024; store bytes as received (client already resized; no server-side re-encode in v1). Upsert semantics. Sets users.avatar_url = "/auth/users/{id}/avatar".
- GET /auth/users/{userID}/avatar (PUBLIC — add to the allowlist in cmd/api/main.go deliberately per CLAUDE.md): serves bytes with correct Content-Type, ETag from updated_at (If-None-Match -> 304), Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600. 404 when none.
- DELETE /auth/avatar (authed): removes the row and nulls users.avatar_url. Idempotent.
- GDPR: DELETE /auth/account cascade must remove user_avatars in the SAME transaction (auth module owns both — direct, no event needed). GET /auth/export includes the avatar as {contentType, data: base64} in the auth section (or a hasAvatar+size note — pick include-it; export is supposed to be complete).
- Profile payloads that feed avatar rendering (own profile, BatchProfiles for friends/feed/public flame) must include avatarUrl so clients get it everywhere without N+1 calls — verify each read path returns it.
- openapi.yaml + generate:api-types.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Integration tests (ConnectParallel): happy (upload->serve roundtrip bytes+content-type, upsert replaces, delete then 404, ETag 304 flow), edge (exactly-512KB boundary, 1024x1024 boundary, webp, re-upload changes ETag), error (oversize, non-image bytes with image content-type, unsupported content-type, unauthenticated PUT/DELETE, avatar of nonexistent user 404).
- TestProductionHashingParamsPinned and all existing auth tests untouched and green; account-deletion test extended to assert avatar row gone.
- Backend gates green.
notes:
EXECUTION STEPS
1. `br show winzy.ai-mfns.1` AND `br show winzy.ai-mfns` (epic context; READ-ONLY, no br writes).
2. Reserve backend/internal/auth/** and backend/migrations/0011_user_avatars.*; backend/cmd/api/main.go (allowlist entry — one quick pass, release fast); openapi.yaml + generated schema.ts only while editing (mtje worker shares them).
3. Migration number is 0011 — 0010 belongs to the concurrent reorder bead; a temporary gap in your working tree is fine, both land together.
4. Verify the image-decoding approach via Context7 BEFORE implementing (image.Decode + golang.org/x/image/webp registration for jpeg/png/webp; confirm current x/image module state). Decode server-side to prove real image + dimensions <= 1024x1024; store bytes as received.
5. Endpoints per spec: PUT /auth/avatar (raw body, content-type allowlist, <=512KB -> auth-style {"errors":{"avatar":[...]}} 400s), GET /auth/users/{id}/avatar (PUBLIC — add the allowlist entry in main.go deliberately, exact path shape), DELETE /auth/avatar (idempotent). users.avatar_url set to the serving path on upload, NULLed on delete.
6. GDPR: extend the existing DeleteAccount transaction (auth owns both tables — direct delete in the same tx, no event needed); export includes {contentType, data base64}. Extend the existing account-deletion + export tests.
7. Read paths: own profile + BatchProfiles must carry avatarUrl (feeds friends/feed/public flame). Verify each and update openapi accordingly.
8. ETag = derived from updated_at; If-None-Match -> 304; Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600. Return the current updated_at (or a version token) in the upload response so the frontend can cache-bust.
9. Package-scoped tests during dev; full gates at end; untouched-package failures -> report, don't fix. openapi + typegen at the very end.
10. Report on this thread, release reservations.
close_reason: Shipped; fix round (decoded-format content type, cascade assert) verified; auth -race green independently; committed

title: Avatar picker and image display across the app

VERIFY both libraries' current APIs via Context7 (expo SDK moves fast) and confirm web support (expo-image-picker web uses a file input — fine).

All 8 call sites pass the avatarUrl from their profile data (ProfileScreen, FriendProfileScreen, FeedScreen, FriendsScreen, AddFriendScreen, SettingsScreen, friends-list components) — the API read paths include avatarUrl per the backend bead.

title: Todo list feature (one-off tasks alongside habits)
description:
From GH issue #138 (deleted 2026-07-16): "Add todo list feature". Gabriel chose FULL SPEC NOW (2026-07-16); this epic is the product spec, children are the implementation.

PRODUCT SPEC (v1):
- Todos are one-off tasks complementing recurring habits: title (1-256 runes), optional due date (local date, no time), done/not-done. Private only — NO social layer, no flame impact, no feed events. Winzy stays habit-first; todos are a convenience.
- Placement: TodayScreen gets a "To-dos" section BELOW the habits list showing open todos due today, overdue, or undated — checkable inline, with quick-add. A quiet "manage" affordance opens a todos overlay for everything else (edit/delete/reorder/completed history).
- Tone per product philosophy: overdue shown gently (subdued "since Mon" tag) — encourage, never punish. No red badges, no nagging.
- Completing keeps the todo (completed_at) and drops it from Today; completed todos live in the overlay's collapsed Completed group (no auto-delete v1). Deleting is hard delete.
- Manual ordering of open todos (position), same explicit-mode interaction spirit as habit reordering.

NON-GOALS (v1): recurring todos (that is what habits are), subtasks, reminders/notifications, sharing, due times.

STRUCTURE (3 children, strictly sequential): .1 backend module -> .2 data layer + Today section + quick-add (also depends on TanStack .1 so this new domain starts on queries, not hand-rolled fetches) -> .3 manage overlay (edit/delete/reorder/completed).

title: Backend todos module (internal/todos)
description:
Backend child of the Todo list epic. A brand-new module — follow the module pattern exactly (this bead doubles as the reference for how to add a module; the events/export wiring below is the checklist).

SPEC:
- Migration (next number): todos — id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), created_at, updated_at, user_id uuid NOT NULL, title varchar(256) NOT NULL, due_date date NULL, position integer NOT NULL, completed_at timestamptz NULL. Index on (user_id). Down migration too.
- New package backend/internal/todos: store.go / service.go / handlers.go / routes.go / validation.go / export.go mirroring the habits module structure.
- Endpoints (all authed, NONE public):
  - POST /todos {title, dueDate?} -> 201 with the todo.
  - GET /todos?status=open|completed|all (default open). Open ordered by position ASC; completed by completed_at DESC.
  - PUT /todos/{id} {title?, dueDate?} — explicit dueDate:null clears the date.
  - POST /todos/{id}/complete — sets completed_at; if already completed, a no-op 200 returning current state (idempotent — there is no data-integrity reason for a conflict here, unlike habit completions).
  - DELETE /todos/{id}/complete — uncompletes; appends to the END of the open list (position = max+1, avoids surprise mid-list insertion); no-op 200 if already open.
  - DELETE /todos/{id} — hard delete.
  - PUT /todos/order {todoIds: [...]} — exact set of the caller's OPEN todos. Malformed input (duplicates, unknown/foreign IDs, completed IDs) -> 400. STALE set (valid IDs but not the complete current open set — happens constantly with multi-device use since todos churn) -> 409 {"error": "Your to-do list changed. Refresh and try again."} so clients can auto-refetch-and-retry (the manage-overlay bead implements that retry).
- Validation: title required, 1-256 RUNES (rune count, not bytes); due_date must parse as YYYY-MM-DD; flat {"error": "..."} habits-style convention (single message, first failure wins). Other users' todos -> 404 (never 403; do not leak existence).
- Position: new todo appended (max+1 per user, computed in the INSERT, no read-then-write race); completing does NOT reposition.
- Module wiring in cmd/api/main.go per the established pattern: todos.NewService(pool, registry, exportRegistry, logger) registering (a) export section "todos" (export.ErrNoData when the user has none -> section omitted) and (b) a UserDeleted cascade handler resolving its querier via db.QuerierFrom(ctx, s.pool) so account deletion removes todos in the SAME transaction (mandatory contract, events/registry.go:22-34). Handlers into the apiHandlers struct + registerAPIRoutes. No events emitted (todos are private; no feed, no flame, no notifications).
- openapi.yaml + npm run generate:api-types.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Full three-area coverage (ConnectParallel, t.Parallel): happy (CRUD + complete/uncomplete + order roundtrip + all three list filters), edge (256-rune title boundary incl. emoji, dueDate set/clear, uncomplete appends at end, complete-when-completed and uncomplete-when-open no-ops, order excludes completed), error (empty/over-length title, bad date, foreign todo 404, order 400 vs 409 cases distinctly tested, unauthenticated).
- Account-deletion integration test extended: user with todos -> DELETE /auth/account -> todos gone, one transaction.
- GET /auth/export includes the todos section for a user with todos and omits it otherwise.
- Backend gates green.
notes:
EXECUTION STEPS
1. `br show winzy.ai-30sf.1` (READ-ONLY — no br write commands ever). Also skim backend/internal/habits/ as the structural template and backend/internal/events/registry.go:1-40 for the cascade contract.
2. Reserve backend/internal/todos/** (new), backend/migrations/<next>_todos.*, and — only while editing, released fast — backend/cmd/api/main.go, the routes wiring file, backend/openapi.yaml, the generated frontend types file (shared with another backend worker; coordinate via PM on conflict).
3. Migration number = next free (ls backend/migrations/). Up + down.
4. Build the module per spec (store/service/handlers/routes/validation/export), then wire main.go in ONE quick compilable pass and release that reservation immediately.
5. Test env: TEST_DATABASE_URL='postgres://winzy:winzy@localhost:5439/winzy?sslmode=disable'. Package-scoped tests during dev (go test -tags=integration -race ./internal/todos/...); extend the account-deletion + export integration tests where they live. Full gates only at the END; failures from untouched packages → report, don't fix.
6. openapi.yaml + npm run generate:api-types at the end.
7. Report on this thread (files, decisions incl. any spec ambiguity you resolved, gate tails, deviations), then release reservations.

title: Todos data layer + Today section + quick-add
description:
Child 2 of the Todo list epic (scope split 2026-07-16: the manage overlay is now child 3). DEPENDS ON the backend todos module bead AND on TanStack child 1 (winzy.ai-d56m.1 — the provider and mutation conventions must exist so this new domain is built on queries from day one, not hand-rolled then migrated).

- Inline checkbox completes with optimistic update; the row checks, lingers a beat, then slides out.

title: Meditation timer
description:
From GH issue #139 (deleted 2026-07-16): "Add meditation timer". Gabriel chose FULL SPEC NOW (2026-07-16); this epic is the product spec.

PRODUCT SPEC (v1):
- A focused, beautiful countdown timer for meditation/breathwork sessions, launched from TodayScreen (small timer icon in the header). Frontend-only feature — no new backend state; the only persistence is (a) logging a habit completion via the existing API and (b) local preferences.
- Session setup: duration presets (5/10/15/20 min) + custom stepper (1-120 min). Start -> full-screen calm countdown (big remaining time, subtle breathing animation, dimmed chrome), pause/resume, end-early (with confirm).
- Completion: gentle end signal — soft chime (small bundled audio asset, respects device mute) + vibration on native + visual bloom. Then a completion moment ("12 minutes of calm") with an optional one-tap "Log to habit" (see integration child).
- Web reality: background tabs throttle timers — compute remaining time from an absolute end timestamp (Date-based math), never by counting intervals; re-sync on visibilitychange so returning to the tab shows the truth; fire the completion state if the end passed while hidden. Keep screen awake during a session on native (expo-keep-awake — verify current API via Context7).
- Tone: encourage, never punish — abandoning a session gets a kind message, no guilt.

NON-GOALS (v1): guided audio content, interval bells, stats/history of sessions (a linked habit's completions ARE the history), background push when tab closed.

Children: timer core first, then habit-logging integration.

title: Meditation timer core (setup, countdown, completion)
description:
Child 1 of the Meditation timer epic — the timer itself, no habit integration yet.

SPEC:
- New overlay id "meditation" (useOverlayRouter + RootNavigator if-chain + OverlayShell wrapper, same as existing overlays); entry point: a small timer icon button in TodayScreen's header (visually quiet — must not compete with habit content).
- Setup view: preset chips 5/10/15/20 min + custom stepper (1-120, step 1); remembers last-used duration (AsyncStorage/localStorage per existing storage util if one exists — check frontend/src/utils/ first).
- Countdown: absolute-end-timestamp math (endsAt = now + duration; remaining derived on each tick; a 500ms interval is only a repaint driver, never the source of truth). Pause stores remaining and clears endsAt; resume re-derives. visibilitychange handler re-syncs and can jump straight to completion if endsAt passed while hidden. Guard timers against unmount leaks.
- During session: big mm:ss, subtle looping breathing animation (reanimated or the existing animation approach in the design system — match what is already used, do not add a new animation dependency), pause/resume button, X to end early with a kind confirm ("End session? Every minute counts.").
- Completion state: soft chime via expo-audio (verify current Expo SDK audio API via Context7 — expo-av was deprecated; confirm what the installed SDK ships), small bundled sound asset (<50KB), Vibration on native, visual bloom; text like "12 minutes of calm". Done button closes the overlay. If audio playback fails (web autoplay policy), fail silently — the visual is the signal.
- Keep-awake during an active session on native via expo-keep-awake (verify API via Context7); no-op on web is fine (screen lock behavior on web PWA is out of scope, do NOT pull in wake-lock hacks for v1).
- Browser-back (once the back-button bead has landed): behaves like any overlay — but an ACTIVE session should get the same kind confirm before being killed. If the back-button bead has not landed yet, just ensure nothing breaks.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Full flow works on web and is native-safe (Platform guards where needed): setup -> countdown -> pause/resume -> completion with chime; custom duration; last-duration remembered.
- Tab hidden through the end moment -> returning shows the completion state with correct elapsed display.
- End-early confirm works; no timer leaks after unmount (test with jest fake timers).
- Component tests for the time math (pause/resume/visibility edge cases) and setup validation; frontend gates green.

Whichever of the three lands last re-evaluates overall density/spacing with the others present. The timer entry must stay visually quiet.

title: Meditation timer to habit-completion logging
description:
Child 2 of the Meditation timer epic. DEPENDS ON the timer core bead.

SPEC:
- On the timer completion screen, offer "Log to habit": a small sheet listing the user's active habits due today (reuse the existing habits data source — TanStack query or hook, whatever is current). Preselect intelligently: (1) the habit the user last logged from the timer (persisted locally), else (2) the first habit whose name matches /medit|breath|mindful|calm|zen/i, else none selected.
- One tap logs a FULL completion for today via the existing POST /habits/{id}/complete contract (timezone in the body, exactly like TodayScreen's toggle — reuse the same mutation/logic path so optimistic behavior, stats refresh, and flame updates come for free; do not duplicate completion plumbing).
- If the chosen habit is already completed today: show it as already-done (checkmark, non-tappable) instead of erroring — never a 409 surfaced to the user.
- Remember the linked habit choice locally (same storage util as the timer's last-duration) and auto-suggest next time.
- Skippable in one tap ("Not now") — logging is optional, zero guilt.
- If the user has no habits at all: show nothing (no upsell in v1).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Complete a session -> log to a due habit -> TodayScreen shows it completed with flame/stats updated, no refresh needed.
- Already-completed habit rendered as done and non-tappable; "Not now" skips cleanly; suggestion prefers last-used, then name match.
- Component tests for the suggestion logic (last-used beats name-match beats none) and the already-completed rendering; frontend gates green.
